title: Women’s cricket: England wins second T20I in Bristol, Three-match series tied 1-1
summary: In Women’s cricket, hosts England defeated India by 26 runs in the second T20 International in Bristol last night. The three-match series is now tied at 1-1. Opting to bat first, England posted 168 for 5 in their allotted 20 overs. For the hosts, Freya Kemp pl...
body: In Women’s cricket, hosts England defeated India by 26 runs in the second T20 International in Bristol last night. The three-match series is now tied at 1-1. Opting to bat first, England posted 168 for 5 in their allotted 20 overs. For the hosts, Freya Kemp played a blistering knock of 39 runs off just 13 balls. For India, left-arm orthodox spinner Shree Charani picked up three wickets. In reply, India could manage only 142 for 9 in their 20 overs. For the visitors, Yastika Bhatia top-scored with 33 runs off 36 balls before retiring hurt. For England, skipper Charlotte Dean was the pick of the bowlers, claiming two wickets at an economy rate of just five runs per over. England’s Freya Kemp was adjudged Player of the Match for her match-winning innings. The third and final T20I of the series will be played on Tuesday in Taunton.

title: Badminton: India’s Satwiksairaj-Chirag win Singapore Open 2026
summary: In Badminton, Indian men’s double pair of Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty won the finals of the Singapore Open tournament today. The Indian pair defeated Indonesian pair of Muhammad Shohibul Fikri and Fajar Alfian 18-21, 21-17, 21-16 in the final mat...
body: In Badminton, Indian men’s double pair of Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty won the finals of the Singapore Open tournament today. The Indian pair defeated Indonesian pair of Muhammad Shohibul Fikri and Fajar Alfian 18-21, 21-17, 21-16 in the final match. This win helped the duo to end their two-year title drought. Earlier, the Indian pair defeated world number one South Korea’s duo Seo Seung-jae and Kim Won-ho, who were on a 34-match winning streak, in the semi-final match.

title: IPL 2026 final: Gujarat Titans and RCB all set for epic title clash today
summary: In IPL Cricket, Gujarat Titans will be up against defending champions Royal Challengers Bengaluru in the final of the 2026 season at Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad today. The match is scheduled to begin at 7:30 PM. Akashvani correspondent reports that Guja...
body: In IPL Cricket, Gujarat Titans will be up against defending champions Royal Challengers Bengaluru in the final of the 2026 season at Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad today. The match is scheduled to begin at 7:30 PM. Akashvani correspondent reports that Gujarat Titans and Royal Challengers Bengaluru will look to make the most of their strengths in the final. Gujarat Titans have beaten RCB at this venue earlier this season, but Bengaluru will draw confidence from having won their maiden IPL title at the same ground last year. RCB have enjoyed an impressive campaign and enter the final after a three-day break. Their batting has been one of the strongest in the tournament, with Virat Kohli leading the charge. In the bowling department, Bhuvneshwar Kumar and Josh Hazlewood have been among their key performers with the new ball. Meanwhile, Gujarat Titans, led by Shubman Gill, secured a top-two finish in the league stage and bounced back from a Qualifier 1 defeat to book their place in the final. The team will once again rely on key batters Gill, Sai Sudharsan and Jos Buttler, while all-rounder Jason Holder has strengthened their bowling attack with important wickets in the second half of the season. Both teams are aiming for their second IPL title. Gujarat won their maiden crown in 2022, while RCB are looking to successfully defend the trophy they won last season.

title: UEFA Champions League: Paris Saint-Germain beat Arsenal 4-3 on penalty shootout in final
summary: In Football, Paris Saint-Germain retained the UEFA Champions League title after defeating Arsenal 4-3 in a penalty shootout in the final in Budapest last night. The match ended 1-1 after extra time. English champions Arsenal took an early lead through Kai Have...
body: In Football, Paris Saint-Germain retained the UEFA Champions League title after defeating Arsenal 4-3 in a penalty shootout in the final in Budapest last night. The match ended 1-1 after extra time. English champions Arsenal took an early lead through Kai Havertz in the sixth minute, but Ballon d’Or winner Ousmane Dembélé equalised from the penalty spot in the 65th minute to force extra time. With the scores still level after 120 minutes, the title was decided on penalties. Arsenal defender Gabriel Magalhaes missed the decisive spot-kick, handing PSG their second successive European crown. The victory makes PSG only the second club in the modern era to successfully defend the Champions League title after Real Madrid. PSG coach Luis Enrique also joined an elite group of managers to win the European Cup or Champions League at least three times. The UEFA Champions League is Europe’s premier club football competition, featuring the top teams from domestic leagues across the continent.

title: Union Ministers Mandaviya and Scindia congratulate Satwiksairaj-Chirag on Singapore Open title win
summary: Youth Affairs and Sports Minister Dr Mansukh Mandaviya and Union Minister Jyotiraditya Scindia congratulated Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty for winning the Singapore Open Super 750 title. In a social media post, Mansukh Mandaviya said that the win e...
body: Youth Affairs and Sports Minister Dr Mansukh Mandaviya and Union Minister Jyotiraditya Scindia congratulated Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty for winning the Singapore Open Super 750 title. In a social media post, Mansukh Mandaviya said that the win ends a two-year wait for the title and is yet another glorious chapter to India’s badminton journey. Dr Mandaviya called this a historic triumph for Indian Badminton. In a social media post, Jyotiraditya Scindia lauded the duo for fighting back from a game down and ending a two-year wait for a title. He said the duo once again demonstrated the resilience, belief and class that make them among the very best in the world.

title: Shubman Gill’s century powers Gujarat Titans into IPL final
summary: In cricket, Gujarat Titans stormed into the final of IPL 2026 with a commanding seven-wicket victory over Rajasthan Royals in Qualifier Two at New Chandigarh. Chasing a formidable target of 215 runs, the 2022 champions produced a batting masterclass, reaching ...
body: In cricket, Gujarat Titans stormed into the final of IPL 2026 with a commanding seven-wicket victory over Rajasthan Royals in Qualifier Two at New Chandigarh. Chasing a formidable target of 215 runs, the 2022 champions produced a batting masterclass, reaching 219 for three in just 18.4 overs. Skipper Shubman Gill led from the front with a scintillating 104 off 53 balls, while Sai Sudharsan contributed 58 runs. Shubman Gill was adjudged Player of the Match. Earlier, after being put in to bat, Rajasthan Royals posted an imposing 214 for six in stipulated 20 overs. Young opener Vaibhav Sooryavanshi starred with a brilliant 96, narrowly missing out on a century again. Ravindra Jadeja remained unbeaten on 45. For Gujarat Titans, Kagiso Rabada and Jason Holder scalped two wickets each. With this victory, Gujarat Titans have set up a title clash against defending champions Royal Challengers Bengaluru. The summit clash will be played in Ahmedabad tomorrow, with the match scheduled to begin at 7:30 PM.

title: India climbs to second spot with eight medals at Asian U-20 Athletics Championships
summary: India continued its impressive performance at the Asian Under-20 Athletics Championships in Hong Kong, taking its medal tally to eight, including four gold, two silver and two bronze medals, and climbing to second place in the standings behind China. On the se...
body: India continued its impressive performance at the Asian Under-20 Athletics Championships in Hong Kong, taking its medal tally to eight, including four gold, two silver and two bronze medals, and climbing to second place in the standings behind China. On the second day of competition yesterday, Indian athletes won seven medals, including four golds. The standout performance came from 19-year-old Pooja Singh, who clinched gold in the women’s high jump with a leap of 1.93 metres. In the process, she broke the 14-year-old senior national record of 1.92 metres, set by Sahana Kumari in 2012. India’s three other gold medals came through Nitin Gupta, who won the men’s 5,000-metre race walk, Amanat Kamboj, who topped the women’s discus throw, and Rahul Jakhar, who bagged gold in the men’s decathlon. Sadhana Devi won silver in the women’s triple jump, while Upkar secured silver in the men’s decathlon. Neeru Pathak added a bronze medal in the women’s 400 metres. India had opened its medal account on Thursday when Nipam claimed bronze in the women’s 100 metres. A 48-member Indian contingent, comprising 30 men and 18 women, is participating in the championships, which will conclude tomorrow.

title: Badminton: Indian shuttlers make way to semifinals of doubles & Mixed doubles
summary: India’s men’s doubles pair, Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty advanced to the semifinals of the Singapore Open Super 750 badminton tournament today. Rankireddy and Chirag beat the Malaysian pair of Aaron Tai and Kang Khai Xing 19-21, 21-17, 21-13. Thei...
body: India’s men’s doubles pair, Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty advanced to the semifinals of the Singapore Open Super 750 badminton tournament today. Rankireddy and Chirag beat the Malaysian pair of Aaron Tai and Kang Khai Xing 19-21, 21-17, 21-13. Their next clash will be against the world number one pair of Seo Seung-jae and Kim Won-ho of South Korea tomorrow. In the mixed doubles, Dhruv Kapila and Tanisha Crasto also managed to enter the semifinals, beating Chen Tang Jie and Toh Ee Wei of Malaysia. In the men’s singles quarterfinal, Lakshya Sen, lost the quarterfinal 19-21, 21-15, 15-21 to Japan’s Koki Watanabe. Veteran shuttler PV Sindhu’s campaign came to an end in the quarterfinals after a hard-fought defeat against world No. 1 An Se Young of South Korea.

title: Rajasthan Royals to take on Gujarat Titans in all-important Qualifier 2 in New Chandigarh today
summary: In IPL Cricket, Gujarat Titans (GT) and Rajasthan Royals (RR) will meet in the all-important Qualifier 2 at the Maharaja Yadavindra Singh International Cricket Stadium, New Chandigarh, this evening. The match will start at 7:30 PM. The winner will take on Roya...
body: In IPL Cricket, Gujarat Titans (GT) and Rajasthan Royals (RR) will meet in the all-important Qualifier 2 at the Maharaja Yadavindra Singh International Cricket Stadium, New Chandigarh, this evening. The match will start at 7:30 PM. The winner will take on Royal Challengers Bengaluru in the IPL 2026 final on Sunday at Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad. Gujarat Titans lost to defending champions Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) in Qualifier 1, while Rajasthan Royals made it by defeating Sunrisers Hyderabad in Eliminator 1. Gujarat will be looking for ways to stop Rajasthan’s opener Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, who had smashed a 28-ball 97 against Sunrisers Hyderabad and will be a big threat to the 2022 winners. Rajasthan had dramatically made the playoffs after beating the Mumbai Indians in their final league phase match at the Wankhede. While the RR, winner of the inaugural edition in 2008, are aiming to make it to the final, GT are determined to reclaim the championship glory they first achieved during their debut season in 2022.

title: HM Amit Shah holds meeting to review preparations for CWG and Olympics to be hosted in Ahmedabad
summary: Union Home Minister Amit Shah held a meeting yesterday to review preparations for the Commonwealth Games (CWG) and proposed Olympics to be hosted in Ahmedabad. Union Sports Minister Mansukh Mandaviya, Gujarat Chief Minister Bhupendra Patel, Deputy Chief Minist...
body: Union Home Minister Amit Shah held a meeting yesterday to review preparations for the Commonwealth Games (CWG) and proposed Olympics to be hosted in Ahmedabad. Union Sports Minister Mansukh Mandaviya, Gujarat Chief Minister Bhupendra Patel, Deputy Chief Minister Harsh Sanghavi and senior officials were present at the meeting. In a social media post, Mr Shah said the Modi government is expanding world-class sports infrastructure and state-of-the-art training facilities in India. Keeping in mind the proposed world-class sporting events in Ahmedabad, sports facilities are being developed in accordance with global standards. In line with this, review meetings were held in Ahmedabad regarding the progress of sports infrastructure and related projects, he said. Ahmedabad would be hosting the 2030 Commonwealth Games and has officially submitted a bid for the 2036 Olympics.

title: SC permits wrestler Vinesh Phogat to participate in selection trials of Asian Games 2026
summary: The Supreme Court today permitted wrestler Vinesh Phogat to participate in the selection trials to be held tomorrow and 31st of this month for the Asian Games 2026. A bench of Justices P S Narasimha and Alok Aradhe passed the order while hearing a plea filed b...
body: The Supreme Court today permitted wrestler Vinesh Phogat to participate in the selection trials to be held tomorrow and 31st of this month for the Asian Games 2026. A bench of Justices P S Narasimha and Alok Aradhe passed the order while hearing a plea filed by the Wrestling Federation of India (WFI) challenging the Delhi High Court order allowing Phogat to participate in the selection trials. A division bench of the high court said, the WFI’s selection policy was exclusionary for the lack of discretion to consider an iconic player like her, who is returning from a maternity break. The high court had said the law must ensure that motherhood does not become a ground to exclude female athletes like Phogat. In her petition before the high court, Phogat has challenged the WFI’s selection policy and circular, which have limited eligibility for participation in the Asian Games trials only to the medal winners of certain tournaments. Phogat has asserted that the qualification window chosen by the WFI substantially overlapped with her notified sabbatical on account of pregnancy and post-partum recovery.

title: PM Modi congratulates Indian wrestlers for outstanding performance at U23 Asian Championships 2026
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi today congratulated Indian wrestlers for their outstanding performance at the 2026 U23 Asian Championships held in Da Nang, Vietnam. In a social media post, Mr Modi said that the Men’s Freestyle and Women’s Wrestling teams won the ...
body: Prime Minister Narendra Modi today congratulated Indian wrestlers for their outstanding performance at the 2026 U23 Asian Championships held in Da Nang, Vietnam. In a social media post, Mr Modi said that the Men’s Freestyle and Women’s Wrestling teams won the Team Titles at the Championships. He said that the Men’s freestyle wrestling team secured a record of nine medals, including four gold medals, registering the country’s highest ever overall medal haul in the history of the championships. The Prime Minister added that the women’s wrestling contingent also delivered a remarkable performance by winning ten medals, including six gold medals. He also highlighted that the Greco-Roman team recorded its best-ever overall medal tally at the championship with eight medals.

title: R Praggnanandhaa defeats Magnus Carlsen in round three of Norway Chess
summary: In Norway Chess, R Praggnanandhaa defeated the Norwegian world No. 1 Magnus Carlsen, gripping the contest to claim three full points in round three of the tournament yesterday. In contrast, world champion D Gukesh continued to endure a difficult run, suffering...
body: In Norway Chess, R Praggnanandhaa defeated the Norwegian world No. 1 Magnus Carlsen, gripping the contest to claim three full points in round three of the tournament yesterday. In contrast, world champion D Gukesh continued to endure a difficult run, suffering another setback after losing to Alireza Firouzja in the final game following a hard-fought classical draw. The victory was another major statement from Praggnanandhaa, who arrived in Oslo on the eve of the Norway Chess after a string of impressive performances at the Superbet Chess Classic Romania in Bucharest. The win took the Indian to 4.5 points and lifted him to the sole second place in the standings at the Norway Chess. He now trails only Firouzja, who leads the tournament with 7.5 points. Meanwhile, Gukesh remains on 3.5 points and continues to struggle for momentum, with his difficult run in the Norway Chess showing little sign of easing. The world champion is currently placed fourth in the six-player Open field.

title: Two-time Paralympic champion Sumit Antil breaks world record in Men’s Javelin Throw F64 in Bengaluru
summary: Two-time Paralympic champion Sumit Antil shattered his own world record in the Men’s Javelin Throw F64 category at 8th Indian Open International Para Athletics Championships 2026 in Bengaluru yesterday, producing a massive throw of 74.82 metres. Representing H...
body: Two-time Paralympic champion Sumit Antil shattered his own world record in the Men’s Javelin Throw F64 category at 8th Indian Open International Para Athletics Championships 2026 in Bengaluru yesterday, producing a massive throw of 74.82 metres. Representing Haryana, Antil delivered a sensational gold medal-winning performance to surpass his previous world record of 73.29m, which he had set at the 2022 Asian Para Games in Hangzhou. Antil comfortably secured the top spot ahead of Maharashtra’s Sandip Sargar, who claimed silver with a throw of 62.88m. Rajasthan’s Sandeep completed the podium with bronze after registering 61.83m. The second day of the championships witnessed several standout performances across track and field events. Paralympic medallist Praveen Kumar cleared 2.00m to clinch gold in the Men’s High Jump T64 event, while fellow Indian para-athlete Varun Singh Bhati secured silver with a clearance of 1.84m. In the Women’s 400m T20 race, Tokyo Paralympic gold medallist Deepthi Jeevanji impressed with a winning time of 57.84 seconds. Maharashtra’s Dilip Gavit also produced a commanding run in the Men’s 400m T47 category, sprinting to gold in 47.64 seconds.

title: PV Sindhu defeats Japan’s Riko Gunji to enter Singapore Open quarterfinals
summary: India enjoyed an impressive day at the Singapore Open today, with Lakshya Sen, PV Sindhu, the men’s doubles pair of Chirag Shetty and Satwiksairaj Rankireddy, and the mixed doubles duo of Dhruv Kapila and Tanisha Crasto all advancing to the quarterfinals. Sind...
body: India enjoyed an impressive day at the Singapore Open today, with Lakshya Sen, PV Sindhu, the men’s doubles pair of Chirag Shetty and Satwiksairaj Rankireddy, and the mixed doubles duo of Dhruv Kapila and Tanisha Crasto all advancing to the quarterfinals. Sindhu defeated Japan’s Riko Gunji in straight games, 21-9, 21-12, in the women’s singles Round of 16. With this victory, Sindhu moves into the quarterfinals, where she is set for a high-profile clash against South Korea’s An Se Young. Lakshya advanced to the quarterfinals after Thailand’s world No. 2 Kunlavut Vitidsarn retired hurt just moments into their Round of 16 clash today. The much-anticipated contest ended abruptly after only two rallies, with Lakshya leading 2-0 in the opening game. Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty have also joined India’s strong quarterfinal lineup after defeating Lee Jhe-Huei and Yang Po-Hsuan 21-15, 11-21, 21-18 in a thrilling three-game Round of 16 clash.

title: Indian contingent all set to play in Under-18 Asia Cup Hockey 2026 in Japan
summary: Indian contingent is all set to play in Under-18 Asia Cup Hockey 2026 beginning in Kakamigahara, Japan, tomorrow to June 6th. India have been assigned to Pool A in the men’s competition, alongside hosts Japan, Korea, Chinese Taipei, and Kazakhstan. They will s...
body: Indian contingent is all set to play in Under-18 Asia Cup Hockey 2026 beginning in Kakamigahara, Japan, tomorrow to June 6th. India have been assigned to Pool A in the men’s competition, alongside hosts Japan, Korea, Chinese Taipei, and Kazakhstan. They will start their campaign against Kazakhstan on May 29, followed by matches against Japan on May 31, Korea on June 1, and Chinese Taipei on June 3. Meanwhile, Pool B includes Malaysia, Pakistan, Bangladesh, and China. The Indian U-18 girls’ team, drawn in Pool A, will begin their campaign against Malaysia on May 30th, followed by matches against Korea on May 31st and Singapore on June 2. Pool B includes China, Japan, Chinese Taipei and Bangladesh. The top two teams from each pool will advance to the semi-finals on June 5th, with the final scheduled for June 6.

title: Rajasthan Royals defeat Sunrisers Hyderabad in IPL eliminator match in New Chandigarh
summary: In IPL Cricket, Rajasthan Royals defeated Sunrisers Hyderabad by 47 runs in the eliminator match in New Chandigarh, last night. Rajasthan Royals set the target of 244 runs against Sunrisers Hyderabad. In reply, Sunrisers Hyderabad could score only 196 runs in ...
body: In IPL Cricket, Rajasthan Royals defeated Sunrisers Hyderabad by 47 runs in the eliminator match in New Chandigarh, last night. Rajasthan Royals set the target of 244 runs against Sunrisers Hyderabad. In reply, Sunrisers Hyderabad could score only 196 runs in 19.2 overs. Put into bat, Rajasthan Royals posted 243 runs for the loss of 8 wickets in stipulated 20 overs. Earlier, Sunrisers Hyderabad won the toss and elected to field. Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has been adjudged as the PLAYER OF THE MATCH. Tomorrow, Rajasthan Royals will take on Gujarat Titans in Qualifier 2, for a place in the final. The final match will be played on Sunday at Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad. Earlier, Royal Challengers Bengaluru stormed into the final beating Gujarat Titans by 92 runs in the Qualifier one match at Dharamshala on Tuesday.

title: India to host 2027 Asian Relays and 2028 Asian Indoor Championships
summary: The Indian cities of Chandigarh and Bhubaneswar were today awarded hosting rights of next year’s Asian Relays and the 2028 Asian Indoor Championships by the continent’s athletics governing body during its executive council meeting in Hong Kong. The Asian Athle...
body: The Indian cities of Chandigarh and Bhubaneswar were today awarded hosting rights of next year’s Asian Relays and the 2028 Asian Indoor Championships by the continent’s athletics governing body during its executive council meeting in Hong Kong. The Asian Athletics Association’s (AAA) council meeting was held ahead of the Asian U20 Championships from May 28 to 31. World Athletics Vice-President Adille Sumariwalla, who attended the AAA council meeting in Hong Kong, said that India will host the Asian Relays in 2027 to be held in Chandigarh and Bhubaneswar will host the Asian Indoor Championships in 2028. The Athletics Federation of India (AFI) had last year submitted the bids to host both the events.

title: Badminton: Indian shuttlers H.S. Prannoy and Lakshya Sen advance to second round of Men’s Singles
summary: In Badminton, Indian shuttler H.S. Prannoy stunned fifth-ranked Jonatan Christie of Indonesia with a 10-21, 21-12, 21-18 first-round victory in Men’s Singles at the Singapore Open today. Lakshya Sen also advanced to the second round beating China’s Lu Guangzu ...
body: In Badminton, Indian shuttler H.S. Prannoy stunned fifth-ranked Jonatan Christie of Indonesia with a 10-21, 21-12, 21-18 first-round victory in Men’s Singles at the Singapore Open today. Lakshya Sen also advanced to the second round beating China’s Lu Guangzu 21-17, 21-15. However, in another Round of 32 match, Kidambi Srikanth lost to Singapore’s Loh Kean Yew 20-22, 21-19, 17-21. Later in the day, Lakshya Sen will take on China’s Lu Guangzu. In the Mixed Doubles, the pair of Dhruv Kapila and Tanisha Crasto defeated the Malaysian duo of Wong Tien Ci and Lim Chiew Sien 21-14, 20-22, 21-13 in the first round. In Women’s Singles, Malvika Bansod exited in the opening round of the tournament after losing to Chinese Taipei’s Lin Hsiang-Ti 21-11, 19-21, 12-21.

title: ISSF WC: Esha Singh breaks World Record in Women’s 25-meter pistol event, clinching gold
summary: Indian shooter Esha Singh today broke the world record in the Women’s 25 metre pistol event at the ISSF World Cup in Munich. 21-year-old Esha earned 43 points from a possible 50 in the final to surpass the previous record of 42 points held by South Korea’s Kim...
body: Indian shooter Esha Singh today broke the world record in the Women’s 25 metre pistol event at the ISSF World Cup in Munich. 21-year-old Esha earned 43 points from a possible 50 in the final to surpass the previous record of 42 points held by South Korea’s Kim Yeji and claimed the gold medal. German shooter Doreen Vennekamp finished second with 38 points, while former world champion Miroslava Mincheva of Bulgaria claimed third place with 31 points.

title: Five-time Olympian shooter Raja Randhir Singh passes away
summary: A renowned shooter, five-time Olympian and a former Honorary Secretary General of the Indian Olympic Association, Raja Randhir Singh passed away at his residence, today, following prolonged illness. He was 79. A veteran sports administrator and cousin of forme...
body: A renowned shooter, five-time Olympian and a former Honorary Secretary General of the Indian Olympic Association, Raja Randhir Singh passed away at his residence, today, following prolonged illness. He was 79. A veteran sports administrator and cousin of former Punjab Chief Minister, now a senior BJP leader, Capt. Amarinder Singh, Randhir Singh belonged to the Royal family of Patiala and was from the fourth generation of the distinguished sports family. His uncle, Maharaja Yadavindra Singh, represented India in Test cricket, while his father, Raja Bhalindra Singh, served as a member of the Indian Olympic Committee from 1947 to 1992. Asian Games gold medalist, former Arjuna awardee, he held several key positions in Indian and international sports administration. He also served as Secretary General of the Olympic Council of Asia and was a member of the International Olympic Committee before becoming an honorary member. He also served on various IOC commissions and represented the IOC on the World Anti-Doping Agency (WADA) Foundation Board. In addition, he held positions with the Sports Authority of India, the Association of National Olympic Committees (ANOC) and the Afro-Asian Games Council, among several other sporting bodies. The National Rifle Association of India (NRAI) has mourned the demise of legendary Shooter and India’s most respected Sports Administrator, Raja Randhir Singh.

title: RCB crush GT by 92 runs to reach IPL final
summary: In IPL Cricket, Royal Challengers Bengaluru stormed into the final beating Gujarat Titans by 92 runs in the Qualifier one match at Dharamshala last night. Chasing a mammoth target of 255 runs, Gujarat Titans were all out for 162 with three balls to spare. Rahu...
body: In IPL Cricket, Royal Challengers Bengaluru stormed into the final beating Gujarat Titans by 92 runs in the Qualifier one match at Dharamshala last night. Chasing a mammoth target of 255 runs, Gujarat Titans were all out for 162 with three balls to spare. Rahul Tewatia was the top scorer with 68 runs. For Royal Challengers Bengaluru, Jacob Duffy took 3 wickets while Bhuvneshwar Kumar, Rasikh Salam Dar and Krunal Pandya took two wickets each. Earlier, put into bat first, Royal Challengers Bengaluru posted a massive 254 runs losing five wickets in the stipulated 20 overs. Rajat Patidar played a captain’s knock scoring unbeaten 93 runs off just 33 balls. Virat Kohli and Krunal Pandya added 43 runs each. For Gujarat Titans, Kagiso Rabada and Jason Holder took two wickets each. Rajat Patidar was named the Player of the Match. The eliminator will be played today between Sunrisers Hyderabad and Rajasthan Royals in New Chandigarh, Mohali at 7:30 PM. The winner will take on Gujarat Titans in Qualifier 2 on Friday for a place in the final, which will be played on 31 of this month at Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad.

title: SHR to face RR in IPL eliminator today
summary: In IPL Cricket, Sunrisers Hyderabad will face Rajasthan Royals in the eliminator match this evening at New Chandigarh, Mohali. The match will start at 7:30 PM. The winner will take on Gujarat Titans in Qualifier 2 on Friday for a place in the final, which will...
body: In IPL Cricket, Sunrisers Hyderabad will face Rajasthan Royals in the eliminator match this evening at New Chandigarh, Mohali. The match will start at 7:30 PM. The winner will take on Gujarat Titans in Qualifier 2 on Friday for a place in the final, which will be played on 31st of this month at Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad. Earlier, Royal Challengers Bengaluru stormed into the final beating Gujarat Titans by 92 runs in the Qualifier one match at Dharamshala last night. Chasing a mammoth target of 255 runs, Gujarat Titans were all out for 162 with three balls to spare.

title: India thrash Maldives 11-nil in opening Group B match of SAFF Women’s Football Championship
summary: In football, India thrashed the Maldives 11-nil in their opening Group B match of the SAFF Women’s Championship in Margao, Goa, yesterday. Aveka Singh starred for the hosts with four goals, while Priyangka Naorem and Karishma scored a brace each. Pyari Xaxa, D...
body: In football, India thrashed the Maldives 11-nil in their opening Group B match of the SAFF Women’s Championship in Margao, Goa, yesterday. Aveka Singh starred for the hosts with four goals, while Priyangka Naorem and Karishma scored a brace each. Pyari Xaxa, Dangmei Grace, and Sangita Basfore also found the net to complete the dominant victory. Ranked 69th in the world, India’s women’s team asserted control from the outset against the 167th-ranked Maldives, rarely allowing their opponents a foothold in the match. The five-time SAFF Women’s Championship winners India will face reigning champions Bangladesh in their next match on Sunday.

title: IPL 2026: Defending champions Royal Challengers Bengaluru to take on Gujarat Titans in Qualifier One today
summary: In IPL playoff cricket, defending champions Royal Challengers Bengaluru will take on Gujarat Titans in Qualifier One in Dharamshala today. The match is scheduled to begin at 7:30 PM. The winner of the match will advance directly to the IPL 2026 final, while th...
body: In IPL playoff cricket, defending champions Royal Challengers Bengaluru will take on Gujarat Titans in Qualifier One in Dharamshala today. The match is scheduled to begin at 7:30 PM. The winner of the match will advance directly to the IPL 2026 final, while the losing side will get another opportunity in Qualifier Two. Tomorrow, Sunrisers Hyderabad will face Rajasthan Royals in the Eliminator in New Chandigarh, Mohali, at 7:30 PM. The losing team will be knocked out of the tournament, while the winner will move on to Qualifier Two. Royal Challengers Bengaluru were the first team to qualify for the playoffs after their victory over Punjab Kings. They finished top of the table with 18 points from 14 matches, securing the number one position on Net Run Rate ahead of Gujarat Titans and Sunrisers Hyderabad, who also ended with 18 points. Rajasthan Royals secured the fourth and final playoff spot with 16 points.

title: Yogasana to be pushed for inclusion in future Olympics, says Sports Minister Mansukh Mandaviya
summary: Youth Affairs and Sports Minister Mansukh Mandaviya today said that Yogasana would be pushed for inclusion in the future Olympic Games roster, keeping in mind India’s bid to host the 2036 Games. Addressing a press conference in New Delhi, Mr Mandaviya said tha...
body: Youth Affairs and Sports Minister Mansukh Mandaviya today said that Yogasana would be pushed for inclusion in the future Olympic Games roster, keeping in mind India’s bid to host the 2036 Games. Addressing a press conference in New Delhi, Mr Mandaviya said that Ahmedabad will host the first World Yogasana Championship from 4th to 8th of next month, marking an important step in positioning the country’s traditional wellness practice as a global sporting discipline. The Minister said Yogasana reflects the country’s soft power and has emerged as a medium for promoting physical fitness, mental well-being and healthy living across the world. Mr Mandaviya said yoga, which originated in India thousands of years ago, is now being widely accepted globally and has become an integral part of daily life in several countries. He said that around 75 countries have registered for the championship and over 5 hundred participants from across the world are expected to take part in the event. He added that the growing international acceptance of yoga presents opportunities for wellness, employment and global outreach. The Minister said Yogasana combines fitness, discipline and mental balance while also creating livelihood opportunities for yoga trainers and wellness professionals. He emphasized that India should take the lead in promoting Yogasana internationally. During the event, the Minister also launched the official mascot, jersey and championship film of the First World Yogasana Championship 2026. The launches highlighted the government’s efforts to promote Yogasana as a global sporting discipline and strengthen awareness about the upcoming international event.

title: Norway Chess 2026: R Praggnanandhaa & D Gukesh win their opening matches
summary: The Norway Chess 2026 started with big action in Oslo. Indian players R Praggnanandhaa and D Gukesh both won their opening matches in Armageddon after draws in classical games. Praggnanandhaa beat Wesley So in the tiebreak. Gukesh also won against Vincent Keym...
body: The Norway Chess 2026 started with big action in Oslo. Indian players R Praggnanandhaa and D Gukesh both won their opening matches in Armageddon after draws in classical games. Praggnanandhaa beat Wesley So in the tiebreak. Gukesh also won against Vincent Keymer. Keymer was close to winning in the classical game, but Gukesh defended well and saved the game. In the Women’s tournament, reigning Women’s World Champion Ju Wenjun and Divya Deshmukh also ended in a draw after a tense battle in which Ju Wenjun was pressing for a win. Divya delivered a strong performance in the Armageddon decider, winning the tiebreak game. Another major result came from Bibisara Assaubayeva, who defeated Indian No. 1 Koneru Humpy in classical chess.

title: Asian Under-23 Wrestling: Mohit Kumar and Chander Mohan clinch gold medals in Men’s Freestyle event
summary: Indian wrestlers continued their impressive performance at the Asian Under-23 Wrestling Championships in Da Nang, Vietnam today, securing two gold, two silver, and one bronze medal in the men’s freestyle category. In the 65 kilogram category, Mohit Kumar clinc...
body: Indian wrestlers continued their impressive performance at the Asian Under-23 Wrestling Championships in Da Nang, Vietnam today, securing two gold, two silver, and one bronze medal in the men’s freestyle category. In the 65 kilogram category, Mohit Kumar clinched the gold medal with a dominant 12-4 victory over Kyrgyzstan’s Oskonbai A. Abdisamatov. Chandermohan also struck gold in the 79 kilogram final after edging past Kazakhstan’s Yerkhan Abil in a thrilling 7-6 contest. Meanwhile, Punit Kumar secured a silver medal in the 92 kilogram category after going down 12-1 to Kyrgyzstan’s Ulukbek Soorombekov in the final. In the 125 kilogram division, Lacky also settled for silver following a 10-0 defeat against Kazakhstan’s Rizabek Aitmukhan in the title clash. In the 86 kilogram category, Sachin Mor bagged the bronze medal by defeating Uzbekistan’s Boburbek Ruzimboev 4-1 in the bronze medal match.

title: Norway Chess 2026 begins in Oslo today with Round one matches
summary: The Norway Chess 2026 kicks off in Oslo, Norway, today with Round one matches. The tournament officially begins at 8:30 PM IST. For the first time in its 13-year history, the super-tournament has moved from Stavanger to the capital city of Oslo, hosting its ma...
body: The Norway Chess 2026 kicks off in Oslo, Norway, today with Round one matches. The tournament officially begins at 8:30 PM IST. For the first time in its 13-year history, the super-tournament has moved from Stavanger to the capital city of Oslo, hosting its matches at the iconic Deichman Bjørvika public library. This will be the 14th edition of Norway Chess and the third edition of Norway Chess Women. The prestigious six-player Open and Women’s categories feature top grandmasters, including Magnus Carlsen, D. Gukesh, and R. Praggnanandhaa.

title: Delhi Capitals defeat Kolkata Knight Riders by 40 runs in IPL at Eden Gardens
summary: In IPL Cricket, Delhi Capitals defeated Kolkata Knight Riders (KKR) by 40 runs at Eden Gardens in Kolkata last night. Delhi Capitals set a target of 204 runs for Kolkata Knight Riders (KKR). In reply, KKR scored only 163 runs in 18.4 overs. Put into bat, Delhi...
body: In IPL Cricket, Delhi Capitals defeated Kolkata Knight Riders (KKR) by 40 runs at Eden Gardens in Kolkata last night. Delhi Capitals set a target of 204 runs for Kolkata Knight Riders (KKR). In reply, KKR scored only 163 runs in 18.4 overs. Put into bat, Delhi Capitals posted 203 runs for the loss of 5 wickets in stipulated 20 overs. Earlier, Kolkata Knight Riders won the toss and opted to bowl. Kuldeep Yadav was declared Player of the Match. Earlier in the day, in another fixture, Rajasthan Royals defeated the host Mumbai Indians by 30 runs at Wankhede Stadium. Put into bat, Rajasthan Royals posted 205 runs for the loss of eight wickets in stipulated 20 overs. In reply, chasing a target of 206 runs, Mumbai Indians managed to score 175 runs for the loss of nine wickets in their allotted 20 overs.

title: India creates history with five medals at International Association of Ultrarunners 24-Hour Asia and Oceania Championships
summary: India created history at the International Association of Ultrarunners 24-Hour Asia and Oceania Championships held in Japan. India won a total of five medals and dominated the men’s category. Amar Singh Devanda won the gold medal after covering an impressive 2...
body: India created history at the International Association of Ultrarunners 24-Hour Asia and Oceania Championships held in Japan. India won a total of five medals and dominated the men’s category. Amar Singh Devanda won the gold medal after covering an impressive 282 point 881 kilometres in 24 hours. Geeno Antony claimed silver with 272 point 894 kilometres, while the bronze also went to India. In the women’s category, the Indian team secured the bronze medal with a combined distance of 667 point 722 kilometres. Tenzin Dolma was the top Indian performer, finishing fourth individually. The championship was held in Hirosaki City, Japan, with 81 athletes from nine countries participating in the endurance event.

title: IPL 2026: Super Sunday double-header to feature crucial matches for playoff qualification today
summary: In IPL Cricket, the “Super Sunday” double-header will feature crucial matches for playoff qualification today. In the first match of the day, Mumbai Indians will take on Rajasthan Royals at Wankhede Stadium in Mumbai at 3.30 pm. For Rajasthan Royals, it is a m...
body: In IPL Cricket, the “Super Sunday” double-header will feature crucial matches for playoff qualification today. In the first match of the day, Mumbai Indians will take on Rajasthan Royals at Wankhede Stadium in Mumbai at 3.30 pm. For Rajasthan Royals, it is a must-win game in their pursuit of a playoff berth. For Mumbai Indians, it was one of the most disappointing seasons in the franchise as they have already been eliminated from IPL 2026. In another clash of the day, Kolkata Knight Riders will face Delhi Capitals at Eden Gardens in Kolkata. The match is scheduled to begin at 7.30 pm. Last night, riding on an unbeaten 101 runs by skipper Shreyas Iyer, Punjab Kings defeated Lucknow Super Giants by 7 wickets at Ekana Cricket Stadium in Lucknow. Chasing a target of 197 runs set by Lucknow Super Giants, Punjab Kings scored 200 for 3 in 18 overs. Prabhsimran Singh made 69 runs. Earlier, Punjab Kings won the toss and opted to bowl.

title: Gurindervir Singh wins men’s 100 metre Gold with new national record at Federation Cup
summary: Gurindervir Singh has clinched the gold medal in the men’s 100m event at the 2026 Athletics Federation Cup, with a new national record timing of 10.09 seconds. He improved his previous personal best of 10.17 seconds and comfortably surpassed the Commonwealth G...
body: Gurindervir Singh has clinched the gold medal in the men’s 100m event at the 2026 Athletics Federation Cup, with a new national record timing of 10.09 seconds. He improved his previous personal best of 10.17 seconds and comfortably surpassed the Commonwealth Games qualification mark of 10.16 seconds. Animesh Kujur secured the silver medal with a timing of 10.20 seconds, while Pranav Pramod took bronze, clocking 10.29 seconds. In the men’s 400m event, Vishal TK won the gold medal with a new national record of 44.98 seconds, becoming the first Indian athlete to break the 45-second barrier in the event. However, he narrowly missed the Commonwealth Games qualification mark of 44.96 seconds by just 0.02 seconds. Rajesh Ramesh claimed the silver medal with a timing of 45.31 seconds, while Jay Kumar bagged the bronze medal after finishing in 45.47 seconds.

title: Sunrisers Hyderabad beat Royal Challengers Bengaluru by 55 runs in IPL
summary: In IPL cricket, Sunrisers Hyderabad defeated Royal Challengers Bengaluru by 55 runs in Hyderabad last night. Batting first after winning the toss, Sunrisers Hyderabad posted a massive 255 for 4 in their stipulated 20 overs. Ishan Kishan was the top scorer with...
body: In IPL cricket, Sunrisers Hyderabad defeated Royal Challengers Bengaluru by 55 runs in Hyderabad last night. Batting first after winning the toss, Sunrisers Hyderabad posted a massive 255 for 4 in their stipulated 20 overs. Ishan Kishan was the top scorer with an unbeaten 79, while Abhishek Sharma made 56 and Heinrich Klaasen contributed 51. For Bengaluru, Rasikh Salam picked up two wickets. In reply, Royal Challengers Bengaluru could manage 200 for 4 in their allotted 20 overs. Rajat Patidar was the top scorer with 56 runs. For Hyderabad, Eshan Malinga claimed two wickets. Ishan Kishan was named Player of the Match. Despite the win, Sunrisers Hyderabad remain in third place on the points table. Royal Challengers Bengaluru continue to hold the top spot due to their stronger overall record and superior points tally. The victory has significantly boosted Sunrisers Hyderabad’s hopes of qualifying for the playoffs, as they are now firmly in contention for a top-four finish. Today, Lucknow Super Giants will take on Punjab Kings in Lucknow at 7:30 this evening.

title: Suraj Vashisht and Sagar Singh reach finals in Greco-Roman categories at Asian U23 Wrestling Championships
summary: In Wrestling, Indian grapplers Suraj Vashisht and Sagar Singh advanced to the finals of the Men’s Greco-Roman categories at the Asian U23 Wrestling Championships in Da Nang, Vietnam today, assuring India of at least two silver medals. In the 60 kg category, Su...
body: In Wrestling, Indian grapplers Suraj Vashisht and Sagar Singh advanced to the finals of the Men’s Greco-Roman categories at the Asian U23 Wrestling Championships in Da Nang, Vietnam today, assuring India of at least two silver medals. In the 60 kg category, Suraj Vashisht edged past Kyrgyzstan’s Nuristan Suiorkulov 3-2 to book his place in the final. He will now take on Aibek Aitbekov of Kazakhstan in the gold medal clash. Meanwhile, in the 67 kg category, Sagar Singh registered a commanding 4-nil victory over Saudi Arabia’s Munthir Abdullah A Jandu in the semifinal. He is set to face Uzbekistan’s Aytjan Khalmakhanov in the final for the gold medal.

title: United World Wrestling postpones 2026 World Wrestling Championships citing geopolitical situation in Gulf region
summary: United World Wrestling (UWW) has postponed the 2026 World Wrestling Championships scheduled in Bahrain from 24th October this year, citing the prevailing geopolitical situation in the Gulf region. The governing body said the decision was taken in coordination ...
body: United World Wrestling (UWW) has postponed the 2026 World Wrestling Championships scheduled in Bahrain from 24th October this year, citing the prevailing geopolitical situation in the Gulf region. The governing body said the decision was taken in coordination with the Bahrain Olympic Committee and Bahrain Wrestling Federation, prioritising the safety and confidence of athletes, officials, delegations and fans. UWW said the early announcement would allow adequate time to identify and prepare a new host nation for the 2026 championships.

title: India’s Ashmita Chaliha advances to Malaysia Masters Super 500 women’s singles quarterfinals
summary: In the Malaysia Masters Super Badminton, India’s Ashmita Chaliha advanced to the women’s singles quarterfinals after a 21-13, 21-16 win over Malaysia’s Goh Jin Wei in the pre-quarterfinals in Kuala Lumpur today. She will next face eighth seed Line Højmark Kjae...
body: In the Malaysia Masters Super Badminton, India’s Ashmita Chaliha advanced to the women’s singles quarterfinals after a 21-13, 21-16 win over Malaysia’s Goh Jin Wei in the pre-quarterfinals in Kuala Lumpur today. She will next face eighth seed Line Højmark Kjaersfeldt of Denmark, who defeated India’s Malvika Bansod 21-16, 8-21, 15-21. Earlier, in the mixed doubles, pair of Sathwik Kanapuram and Radhika Sharma went down 12-21, 25-27 to Americans Presley Smith and Jennie Gai. In men’s doubles, Hariharan Amsakarunan and MR Arjun lost to China’s Hu Ke Yuan and Lin Xiang Yi.

title: India U-18 Men end series with 3-3 draw against Australia
summary: India Under-18 Men’s Hockey Team played a 3-3 draw against Australia in the fourth and final match of the series at the Udhav Das Mehta (Bhai Ji) Central SAI Centre in Bhopal, Madhya Pradesh, yesterday. With this, Indian men finish the four-match series agains...
body: India Under-18 Men’s Hockey Team played a 3-3 draw against Australia in the fourth and final match of the series at the Udhav Das Mehta (Bhai Ji) Central SAI Centre in Bhopal, Madhya Pradesh, yesterday. With this, Indian men finish the four-match series against Australia with one win, two draws, and one loss. In the last match of the series, India started strongly and scored two early goals, but Australia fought back and equalised in the second quarter. After a closely fought third quarter, India regained the lead with a field goal. However, Australia found another equaliser late in the game as scored a field goal with just three minutes remaining. For India, captain Ketan Kushwaha, Karan Gautam and Ben Siddharth netted goals. Earlier in the day, the India Under-18 Women’s Hockey Team produced a dominant display to defeat the Australia Under-18 Women’s Team 4-1 in the fourth and final match of the series at the same venue. Irrespective of the win, Australia won the four-match series 4-1. In the fourth match, Australia took the lead early in the first quarter, but Indian captain Sweety Kujur responded quickly, scoring the equaliser before the end of the opening quarter. The hosts continued their momentum when Diya scored a goal in the second quarter and took the lead to give India a 2-1 advantage heading into halftime. India maintained control in the second half and extended their lead in the third quarter through Priyanka Minz, while Nausheen Naz added another goal in the final quarter to cap off a dominant win for the hosts.

title: IPL 2026: Kolkata Knight Riders beat Mumbai Indians by 4 wickets to keep their Playoff hopes alive
summary: In IPL Cricket, Kolkata Knight Riders defeated Mumbai Indians by 4 wickets at their home ground Eden Gardens last night to keep Playoff hopes alive. Chasing a target of 148 runs in a rain-affected match, Kolkata Knight Riders overhauled the target with 4 wicke...
body: In IPL Cricket, Kolkata Knight Riders defeated Mumbai Indians by 4 wickets at their home ground Eden Gardens last night to keep Playoff hopes alive. Chasing a target of 148 runs in a rain-affected match, Kolkata Knight Riders overhauled the target with 4 wickets in-hand and 7 balls to go. Manish Pandey scored highest 45 runs, while Rovman Powell contributed 40 runs in the chase. Manish Pandey was adjudged Player of the Match. For Mumbai Indians, Corbin Bosch bagged 3 wickets. Earlier, after being invited to bat first, Mumbai Indians scored 147 for 8 in stipulated 20 overs. Corbin Bosch scored highest 32 runs for Mumbai Indians. On the other hand, bowlers of KKR dominated the 22 yards as Saurabh Dubey, Cameron Green and Kartik Tyagi each picked up 2 wickets. In today’s fixture, Gujarat Titans will take on Chennai Super Kings at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad. The match will begin at 07:30 PM. Gujarat Titans have already qualified for the Playoffs, while the Chennai Super Kings are eying for the fourth spot in the points table.

title: Indian Pickleball Association to hold Sub-Junior and Junior World Cup trials in Ahmedabad
summary: The Indian Pickleball Association (IPA) will be conducting trials for the Indian Sub-Junior and Junior teams for the upcoming Pickleball World Cup from June 12 to 14. The three-day trials in Ahmedabad will determine the junior squad for the third edition of th...
body: The Indian Pickleball Association (IPA) will be conducting trials for the Indian Sub-Junior and Junior teams for the upcoming Pickleball World Cup from June 12 to 14. The three-day trials in Ahmedabad will determine the junior squad for the third edition of the Pickleball World Cup, sanctioned by the Global Pickleball Federation, which will be held in Vietnam from August 30 to September 9. Over 100 players from across the country, including Delhi, Maharashtra, West Bengal, Assam, Rajasthan, Odisha, Haryana, and Gujarat, are competing for a chance to represent India in the U14 and U18 categories at the World Cup. The selection committee for the trials include Aalap Sharma, Dhiren Patel, Mohit Kumar, Atul Edward and IPA CEO, Aditya Khanna. Notable names set to feature in the trials include Arjun Singh, who recently clinched three medals in singles and doubles at the US Open. Also in contention is Naomi Amalsadiwala, regarded as one of India’s rising stars in the open category. Panth Thakkar, who secured a doubles bronze at the US Open, along with Purvansh Patel, Anoushka Chabbria, Veer Shah and Dev Shah, will also compete for spots in the Indian team.

title: Preliminary squad for SAFF Women’s Championship 2026 announced
summary: In Football, the preliminary squad for the SAFF Women’s Championship 2026 has been announced. Senior women’s national team head coach Crispin Chettri announced the 26-member squad yesterday. The tournament will be held at Pandit Jawaharlal Nehru Stadium from 2...
body: In Football, the preliminary squad for the SAFF Women’s Championship 2026 has been announced. Senior women’s national team head coach Crispin Chettri announced the 26-member squad yesterday. The tournament will be held at Pandit Jawaharlal Nehru Stadium from 25th May to 6th June. The final squad of 23 players will be announced closer to the tournament. Placed in Group B, India will open their campaign against Maldives on the opening day before taking on Bangladesh on 31st May. Group A comprises Nepal, Sri Lanka and Bhutan. The top two teams from each group will qualify for the semifinals, scheduled to be held on 3rd June. The final will take place on 6th June.

title: Virat Kohli praises fitness standards of Indian hockey players
summary: Senior Indian Cricketer and legendary batter Virat Kohli has praised the fitness standards of Indian Hockey players, calling it a recognition of the sport’s demands and dedication. Virat Kohli said this during the third edition of the RCB Innovation Lab Indian...
body: Senior Indian Cricketer and legendary batter Virat Kohli has praised the fitness standards of Indian Hockey players, calling it a recognition of the sport’s demands and dedication. Virat Kohli said this during the third edition of the RCB Innovation Lab Indian Sports Summit yesterday. Kohli said, Cricket is in such prime focus in our country that we often overlook other sports. He further said that Cricketers are not even 15 per cent of the fitness of an Indian hockey player. Hockey India, in a statement, said that Virat Kohli’s comments have resonated deeply within the Indian hockey fraternity, with players viewing the praise not just as a compliment, but as recognition of the relentless work and discipline demanded by modern hockey. Reacting to Kohli’s comments, Indian Men’s Hockey Captain Harmanpreet Singh recalled a recent interaction with the cricket icon and said his words carried great significance for the sport. Veteran hockey player Manpreet Singh and midfielder Hardik Singh echoed similar sentiments.

title: Sunrisers Hyderabad defeat Chennai Super Kings by 5 wickets in Chennai
summary: In IPL cricket, Sunrisers Hyderabad defeated Chennai Super Kings by five wickets in Chennai last night to secure a playoff berth. Put into bat first, the Chennai Super Kings posted 180 for seven in 20 overs, with Kartik Sharma being the highest scorer with 44 ...
body: In IPL cricket, Sunrisers Hyderabad defeated Chennai Super Kings by five wickets in Chennai last night to secure a playoff berth. Put into bat first, the Chennai Super Kings posted 180 for seven in 20 overs, with Kartik Sharma being the highest scorer with 44 runs. For Hyderabad, Pat Cummins picked up three wickets, while Sakib Hussain took two. In reply, Sunrisers Hyderabad overhauled the target, losing five wickets in 19 overs, riding on Ishan Kishan’s 70 and Heinrich Klaasen’s 47 runs. Ishan Kishan was declared the Player of the Match. Following the match, Sunrisers Hyderabad now have 16 points with a game in hand, which is enough to ensure their place in the playoffs. The Gujarat Titans, also with 16 points, have marched into the knockouts. Royal Challengers Bengaluru, with 18 points, have already sealed their place in the playoffs. The Super Kings, with 12 points from 13 matches, are not yet completely out of the race for the knockouts. Today, the Rajasthan Royals will face Lucknow Super Giants in Jaipur at 7:30 PM.

title: ICC announces star-studded commentary panel for Women’s T20 World Cup
summary: The International Cricket Council (ICC) has announced a star-studded list of commentators for the upcoming Women’s T20 World Cup in England and Wales. The female-led commentary team features several Indian names, including former captain and international star...
body: The International Cricket Council (ICC) has announced a star-studded list of commentators for the upcoming Women’s T20 World Cup in England and Wales. The female-led commentary team features several Indian names, including former captain and international stars like Mithali Raj, Anjum Chopra, and Veda Krishnamurthy. Dinesh Karthik will join the commentary box as a male counterpart, as also India’s Jatin Sapru. The 10th edition of the Women’s T20 World Cup will kickstart on Friday with hosts England facing Sri Lanka in the opener at Edgbaston in Birmingham.

title: India beat West Indies by 26 runs in Women’s T20 World Cup warm-up match
summary: In Women’s Cricket, India defeated the West Indies by 26 runs in their first warm-up match ahead of the ICC Women’s T20 World Cup in Cardiff, UK yesterday. After being invited to bat first, Harmanpreet Kaur-led Women in Blue posted 179 for 8 in the stipulated ...
body: In Women’s Cricket, India defeated the West Indies by 26 runs in their first warm-up match ahead of the ICC Women’s T20 World Cup in Cardiff, UK yesterday. After being invited to bat first, Harmanpreet Kaur-led Women in Blue posted 179 for 8 in the stipulated 20 overs, powered by Bharti Fulmali’s unbeaten 56 runs off 40 balls. Star batter Smriti Mandhana contributed 39 runs. In reply, the West Indies could only score 153 for 8 in 20 overs. Indian spinner Shreyanka Patil bagged 4 wickets, while Radha Yadav picked up 3. India will next face hosts England in their next warm-up fixture tomorrow. The Women in Blue will kick off their T-20 World Cup campaign against arch-rivals Pakistan at Edgbaston on Sunday.

title: India crushes Afghanistan by an innings and 300 runs, registers biggest-ever victory in Test cricket
summary: In cricket, India registered their biggest-ever victory in Test cricket, crushing Afghanistan by an innings and 300 runs on the third day of the one-off Test at the Maharaja Yadavindra Singh International Cricket Stadium in New Chandigarh today. After declarin...
body: In cricket, India registered their biggest-ever victory in Test cricket, crushing Afghanistan by an innings and 300 runs on the third day of the one-off Test at the Maharaja Yadavindra Singh International Cricket Stadium in New Chandigarh today. After declaring their first innings at 564/8, India bowled Afghanistan out for 152 before lunch on Day 3 and enforced the follow-on. The visitors were then dismissed for 112 in 35.5 overs, handing India a record victory inside three days. India laid the foundation for the emphatic win with a dominant batting display. KL Rahul scored 100, while captain Shubman Gill led from the front with 126. Sai Sudharsan and Rishabh Pant contributed 81 runs each, and Washington Sundar remained unbeaten on 52 as India posted a mammoth total. With the ball, debutant left-arm spinner Manav Suthar starred with figures of 6/33 in Afghanistan’s first innings, while Washington Sundar claimed 4/36 in the second innings to complete India’s comprehensive triumph. The victory surpassed India’s previous largest Test win by margin of runs and marked the most dominant result in the team’s Test history.

title: Indian wrestlers win 2 gold and 2 bronze in Men’s freestyle category at Ulaanbaatar Open in Mongolia
summary: Indian wrestlers continued their impressive run at the Ulaanbaatar Open in Mongolia today, winning two gold and two bronze medals in the men’s freestyle category. Deepak clinched the gold medal in the 61kg category, defeating Kazakhstan’s Assyl Aitakyn 6-Nil i...
body: Indian wrestlers continued their impressive run at the Ulaanbaatar Open in Mongolia today, winning two gold and two bronze medals in the men’s freestyle category. Deepak clinched the gold medal in the 61kg category, defeating Kazakhstan’s Assyl Aitakyn 6-Nil in the final. Dinesh struck gold, beating Kazakhstan’s Yedige Kassimbek 2-Nil in the 125kg final. Meanwhile, Mohit Kumar secured a bronze medal in the 65kg category after registering a commanding 17-6 victory over Kazakhstan’s Ossimzhan Dastanbek. In the 97kg category, Vicky bagged bronze, defeating Mongolia’s Gankhuyag Ganbaatar 4-Nil.

title: Cricket: Afghanistan trails against India by 451 runs in first innings at Stumps on 2nd day of one-off Test
summary: In Cricket, Afghanistan were 113 for 5 in their first innings at stumps on Day Two of the one-off Test against India in New Chandigarh, trailing by 451 runs. Rahmat Shah remained unbeaten on 43 at the end of play. For India, Debutant Manav Suthar picked up thr...
body: In Cricket, Afghanistan were 113 for 5 in their first innings at stumps on Day Two of the one-off Test against India in New Chandigarh, trailing by 451 runs. Rahmat Shah remained unbeaten on 43 at the end of play. For India, Debutant Manav Suthar picked up three wickets, while fast bowler Prasidh Krishna claimed two. Earlier in the day, India declared their first innings at a commanding 564 for 8. Captain Shubman Gill led the charge with 126, while KL Rahul struck 100. Rishabh Pant and Sai Sudharsan contributed 81 runs each, and Washington Sundar remained unbeaten on 52. For Afghanistan, Saleem Safi was the standout bowler, taking six wickets. Yesterday, India had won the toss and elected to bat first. The one-off Test is being played outside the World Test Championship cycle.

title: India defeats Bangladesh and lifts SAFF Women’s Championship 2026 trophy for record sixth time
summary: In the SAFF Women’s Championship 2026 final, India defeated the defending champions, Bangladesh, 3-1 to win the title for a record sixth time. The match was played in Madgaon, Goa, yesterday. After seven years, the Blue Tigresses have once again lifted the tro...
body: In the SAFF Women’s Championship 2026 final, India defeated the defending champions, Bangladesh, 3-1 to win the title for a record sixth time. The match was played in Madgaon, Goa, yesterday. After seven years, the Blue Tigresses have once again lifted the trophy. “In the SAFF Women’s Championship 2026 final played in Madgaon, Goa, India defeated Bangladesh 3-1 to win the title for a record sixth time. With this victory, the Indian women’s team has brought the prestigious trophy back after seven years. From the very start of the final, both teams played with strong intensity. In the first half, India took the lead through a goal by Pyari Xaxa. However, Bangladesh equalised with a goal from Ritu Porna Chakma. At half-time, the score was 1-1. In the second half, India dominated the game. Sanfida scored a brilliant goal to put India ahead once again, and Linda Kom added the third goal to seal the win. After that, Bangladesh could not make a comeback, and India won the match 3-1. Throughout the tournament, the Indian team showed excellent performance, and they continued their strong form in the final. The Blue Tigresses ended their campaign with a memorable victory.

title: Union Minister Mansukh Mandaviya participates in 77th Fit India Sundays on Cycle initiative
summary: Union Youth Affairs and Sports Minister Dr Mansukh Mandaviya today participated in the 77th edition of the “Fit India Sundays on Cycle” initiative in New Delhi this morning. Talking to the media, the Minister said that cycling keeps the planet clean and offers...
body: Union Youth Affairs and Sports Minister Dr Mansukh Mandaviya today participated in the 77th edition of the “Fit India Sundays on Cycle” initiative in New Delhi this morning. Talking to the media, the Minister said that cycling keeps the planet clean and offers a solution to the major problem of traffic congestion. Mentioning several health benefits of Cycling, Dr Mandaviya urged citizens to join the initiative and embrace the vision of a ‘Fit India’. Actor Vikrant Massey, who also participated in the Fit India Sundays on Cycle, described the initiative as incredible and said it has now become a movement. He added that the nation can only become developed when its citizens are fit and noted that this is a generational commitment that we all need to take and pass on to the coming generations.

title: Asian Games-bound Sagar Jaglan and Kajal Dhochak clinch golds at Ulaanbaatar Open 2026
summary: Asian Games-bound Sagar Jaglan and teenager Kajal Dhochak clinched golds in their respective categories as India secured six more medals on the third day of the Ulaanbaatar Open 2026 wrestling tournament in Mongolia yesterday. The 21-year-old Sagar Jaglan topp...
body: Asian Games-bound Sagar Jaglan and teenager Kajal Dhochak clinched golds in their respective categories as India secured six more medals on the third day of the Ulaanbaatar Open 2026 wrestling tournament in Mongolia yesterday. The 21-year-old Sagar Jaglan topped the podium in the men’s freestyle 74kg category with a 3-2 win over Tamir Eshinimaev in the final. The Indian wrestler began his campaign with a 7-2 win over compatriot Jaideep in the quarter-finals. Sagar then beat Mongolia’s Tolui Munkhbat 10-0 by technical superiority in the semi-finals. The 22-year-old Jaideep, meanwhile, recovered through repechage to claim the bronze after a 15-4 technical superiority win over Munkhbat in the medal bout. In women’s wrestling, U23 Asian champion Kajal struck gold in the 76kg category after defeating Nigeria’s Damola Ojo – an African champion – 8-0 in the final. The 18-year-old Kajal opened her campaign with a victory by fall against Kazakhstan’s Gulmaral Yerkebayeva in the qualification round before overpowering the People’s Republic of China’s Saihan Ao 12-1 in the quarter-finals. She then defeated Kazakhstan’s Elmira Syzdykova by fall in the semi-finals to enter the gold medal match. Asian Games-bound wrestler Priya Malik settled for a bronze medal in the same category after beating Mongolia’s Tuvshinjargal Tarav 8-0. Priya had lost 5-1 against Damola Ojo in the semis to enter the bronze medal bout. India’s Hansika Lamba also secured bronze in the women’s 55kg category after registering a 10-0 technical superiority win over Mongolia’s Ariunzaya Odonchimeg. Mansi Ahlawat, part of India’s Asian Games 2026 wrestling squad, also bagged a bronze in the women’s 62kg category. The 25-year-old Indian wrestler defeated compatriot Nitika 10-0 by technical superiority to land on the podium.

title: India clinch Men’s U-18 Asia Cup title, thrash Japan 4-1 in final
summary: Indian Men’s Hockey team has lifted the Under-18 Asia Cup for the third time, thrashing hosts Japan 4-1 in the final in Kakamigahara on Saturday. For India, Ashish Purti starred with a hat-trick as skipper Ketan Kushwaha scored the other goal. Gaku Numada scor...
body: Indian Men’s Hockey team has lifted the Under-18 Asia Cup for the third time, thrashing hosts Japan 4-1 in the final in Kakamigahara on Saturday. For India, Ashish Purti starred with a hat-trick as skipper Ketan Kushwaha scored the other goal. Gaku Numada scored the only goal for the hosts. Meanwhile, Indian Women’s team clinched bronze after beating South Korea 3-0. President Droupadi Murmu has congratulated the Indian Under-18 men’s and women’s hockey teams for their commendable performance at the U-18 Asia Cup 2026. In a social media post, the President said that the men’s team has secured the championship title, while the women’s team has won the Bronze Medal, bringing pride to the nation. She said that these results reflect the dedication, discipline, and determination of country’s young athletes. The President noted that the outstanding performances of the two teams would serve as an inspiration for the youth of the country. Vice President CP Radhakrishnan congratulated the Indian Men’s U-18 Hockey Team for winning the Under-18 Asia Cup 2026 and the Women’s Under-18 Hockey Team for securing the Bronze Medal in the tournament. In a social media post, Mr Radhakrishnan said, these achievements are a testament to the talent, hard work and determination of the country’s young athletes. The Vice President also expressed hope that the success of the young players would inspire many aspiring sports persons across the country and pave the way for even greater accomplishments in the years ahead. Prime Minister Narendra Modi has also congratulated Indian Men’s Under-18 Hockey Team on winning the Asia Cup 2026. In a social media post, Mr Modi said, the team displayed exceptional skill and teamwork throughout the tournament, culminating in a memorable victory in the final. He said, it is a splendid accomplishment by the young hockey players. The Prime Minister said, this triumph also reflects hockey’s growing popularity among the youth. He conveyed best wishes to the team for their future endeavours. The Prime Minister also congratulated Indian Women’s Under-18 Hockey Team on securing the Bronze Medal at the U-18 Asia Cup 2026. He said, the team displayed remarkable grit throughout the tournament. Mr Modi said, this feat reflects the growing strength of Women’s hockey in India and the immense potential of the players. He wished the team the very best for their upcoming endeavours.

title: India tops medal table with 7 medals at World Yogasana Sports Championship 2026
summary: In the World Yogasana Sports Championship 2026, India’s Rohan Tayde won gold medal at Ahmedabad on Saturday. He achieved this achievement in the boys’ sub-junior artistic single competition. He expressed happiness after winning the medal. So far, India has won...
body: In the World Yogasana Sports Championship 2026, India’s Rohan Tayde won gold medal at Ahmedabad on Saturday. He achieved this achievement in the boys’ sub-junior artistic single competition. He expressed happiness after winning the medal. So far, India has won a total of 7 medals, including six gold, in this world-class yoga competition. Yesterday, India’s sub-junior boys’ traditional group team defeated Nepal to win the gold medal. India’s junior girls’ traditional group team also defeated Nepal to win the gold medal. India has maintained its top position in the medal table.

title: Savitha Shri Baskar wins 2026 Women’s Asian Individual Chess Championship
summary: Nineteen-year-old Savitha Shri Baskar has won the 2026 Women’s Asian Individual Chess Championship in Ulaanbaatar, Mongolia. With the win, she also secured qualification for the 2027 FIDE Women’s World Cup. The Chennai-based International Master finished with ...
body: Nineteen-year-old Savitha Shri Baskar has won the 2026 Women’s Asian Individual Chess Championship in Ulaanbaatar, Mongolia. With the win, she also secured qualification for the 2027 FIDE Women’s World Cup. The Chennai-based International Master finished with 7.5 points from nine rounds, edging Afruza Khamdamova of Uzbekistan on tie-breaks. Song Yuxin from China took bronze with 6.5 points. The triumph is the biggest achievement of Savitha’s career and gives India its 13th Women’s Asian Championship title, with the country now winning three of the last four editions.

title: India reach 368/3 against Afghanistan at stumps on Day One
summary: In the One-off cricket test against Afghanistan, India were 368 for 3 in their first innings at stumps on Day One in New Chandigarh. Centuries from KL Rahul and captain Shubman Gill put hosts in a strong position. Sai Sudharsan also contributed 81 before missi...
body: In the One-off cricket test against Afghanistan, India were 368 for 3 in their first innings at stumps on Day One in New Chandigarh. Centuries from KL Rahul and captain Shubman Gill put hosts in a strong position. Sai Sudharsan also contributed 81 before missing out on a maiden Test hundred, while Rishabh Pant remained unbeaten with a quick half-century. For Afghanistan, Mohammad Saleem claimed two wickets. Whereas Ziaur Rahman took one wicket. Earlier, Indian skipper won the toss and opted to bat first. For India, all rounder Manav Suthar from Rajasthan is making his debut for the Test side. On the other hand, Afghanistan have also handed a Test debut to spin-bowling all rounder Nangeyalia Kharote. The One-off test match operates outside the World Test Championship cycle.

title: BCCI announces India’s T20I squad for England & Ireland tour; Shreyas Iyer named captain
summary: The Board of Control for Cricket in India (BCCI) today announced Team India’s 16-member T20 International squad for the tours of England and Ireland. Shreyas Iyer has been appointed as India’s new T20I captain, replacing Suryakumar Yadav. Moreover, Tilak Varma...
body: The Board of Control for Cricket in India (BCCI) today announced Team India’s 16-member T20 International squad for the tours of England and Ireland. Shreyas Iyer has been appointed as India’s new T20I captain, replacing Suryakumar Yadav. Moreover, Tilak Varma has been named as Vice Captain. The BCCI has also included young sensation Vaibhav Sooryvanshi in the squad for the first time, following his great IPL 2026 season, scoring highest runs. Prince Yadav has also been added to the squad. The team was announced after a meeting of Team India’s Men’s Selection Committee, led by Chief Selector Ajit Agarkar and BCCI Secretary Devajit Saikia, for deciding the squad for the tours of Ireland and England and the Asian Games in Japan, as well as India A’s tour of Sri Lanka (multi-day games) at the BCCI Headquarters in Mumbai today.

title: Indian Grandmaster, R Praggnanandhaa scripts history; Becomes first Indian to win Norway Chess title
summary: In chess, Grandmaster R. Praggnanandhaa has created history by becoming the first Indian to win the Norway Chess title in Oslo, with a final-round victory over Germany’s Vincent Keymer. Praggnanandhaa, who began the day in third place, secured a crucial classi...
body: In chess, Grandmaster R. Praggnanandhaa has created history by becoming the first Indian to win the Norway Chess title in Oslo, with a final-round victory over Germany’s Vincent Keymer. Praggnanandhaa, who began the day in third place, secured a crucial classical win worth three points to finish with 18 points and seal the title. The 20-year-old from Chennai achieved a feat that had eluded Indian chess legend Viswanathan Anand and reigning world champion D. Gukesh since the tournament began in 2013. Competing in Norway Chess for only the second time, Praggnanandhaa produced a strong finish, including two classical wins over world number one Magnus Carlsen during the event. American Grandmaster Wesley So, who led the standings going into the final round, finished second with 17 points after winning the Armageddon tie-break against Alireza Firouzja, who ended third with 15.5 points. D. Gukesh had a disappointing campaign, while Carlsen finished fourth with 13 points after losing to Praggnanandhaa in the final round.

title: Indian wicket-keeper, batter KS Bharat announces retirement from international cricket
summary: Indian wicket-keeper and batter KS Bharat has announced his retirement from international cricket. He represented India at Test level on seven occasions, with his last appearance coming against England at Visakhapatnam in February 2024. The 32-year-old made 19...
body: Indian wicket-keeper and batter KS Bharat has announced his retirement from international cricket. He represented India at Test level on seven occasions, with his last appearance coming against England at Visakhapatnam in February 2024. The 32-year-old made 19 dismissals as keeper. Bharat was also part of India’s XI for the ICC World Test Championship Final against Australia at The Oval in June 2023.

title: India beat Bangladesh 3–1 to win sixth SAFF Women’s Championship title
summary: India thrashed Bangladesh 3-1 to clinch a record-extending sixth SAFF Women’s Championship 2026 title at Margao, Goa, on Saturday. Pyari Xaxa, Lynda Kom Serto and Sanfida Nongrum scored a goal each for India, while Ritu Porna Chakma netted the lone goal for Ba...
body: India thrashed Bangladesh 3-1 to clinch a record-extending sixth SAFF Women’s Championship 2026 title at Margao, Goa, on Saturday. Pyari Xaxa, Lynda Kom Serto and Sanfida Nongrum scored a goal each for India, while Ritu Porna Chakma netted the lone goal for Bangladesh. Earlier, India secured their place in the final with a 1-0 victory over Bhutan in the semifinals.

title: PM Modi urges World Yogasana Championship participants to become ambassadors of ‘Yoga 365’ campaign
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has called upon athletes participating in the ‘World Yogasana Championship’ to become global ambassadors of the Ayush Ministry’s ‘Yoga 365’ campaign when they return to their home countries. Speaking at the opening ceremony of the ...
body: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has called upon athletes participating in the ‘World Yogasana Championship’ to become global ambassadors of the Ayush Ministry’s ‘Yoga 365’ campaign when they return to their home countries. Speaking at the opening ceremony of the championship in Ahmedabad, Mr Modi said the Ministry of AYUSH has launched the ‘Yoga 365’ campaign to encourage year-round engagement with yoga and promote healthier lifestyles across communities. Prime Minister urged athletes from across the world to help spread awareness about yoga beyond the championship. The appeal came as Ahmedabad hosted the first World Yogasana Championship, which has attracted more than 400 competitors from over 60 countries. The event is being held from June 4 to 8 and is regarded as a landmark effort to develop Yogasana as a structured international sport. Mr Modi said yoga’s appeal extended beyond physical fitness and offered a practical approach to preventive health. Prime Minister said the championship represented more than a sporting competition and offered an opportunity to spread the broader message of yoga worldwide. The Ayush Ministry has been at the forefront of government initiatives aimed at expanding yoga participation in India and internationally. The World Yogasana Championship has also received support from the Ministry alongside sports authorities and organising bodies involved in promoting Yogasana globally.

title: World Yogasana: India maintains top position with five gold
summary: India has won a total of 6 medals, including five gold, in the World Yogasana Championship 2026. India’s Sub Junior Boys Traditional Group team defeated Nepal to win the gold medal. While India’s Junior Girls Traditional Group team also defeated Nepal to win t...
body: India has won a total of 6 medals, including five gold, in the World Yogasana Championship 2026. India’s Sub Junior Boys Traditional Group team defeated Nepal to win the gold medal. While India’s Junior Girls Traditional Group team also defeated Nepal to win the gold medal. India has maintained its top position in the medal table .

title: U-18 Asia Cup Hockey: India enter final beating Pakistan 5-3 in Semi Final
summary: In Hockey, India defeated Pakistan 5-3 in the Semifinal of the Men’s Under-18 Asia Cup in Kakamigahara, Japan to set up a title clash against the hosts. In a high-scoring contest, India held its nerve against its arch-rival to seal its place in the final. Japa...
body: In Hockey, India defeated Pakistan 5-3 in the Semifinal of the Men’s Under-18 Asia Cup in Kakamigahara, Japan to set up a title clash against the hosts. In a high-scoring contest, India held its nerve against its arch-rival to seal its place in the final. Japan booked its place in the final with a commanding 8-1 win over Malaysia in the first semifinal. Meanwhile Indian women’s team made an exit from the Under-18 Asia Cup losing to China in penalty shootouts in the semifinal today.

title: Sri Lanka’s Rumesh Tharanga becomes fourth Asian to break 90m mark in Javelin; sets new national record
summary: Sri Lanka’s Rumesh Tharanga Pathirage produced a stunning performance at the Rome Diamond League, becoming the fourth Asian athlete to breach the 90 metre mark as he won the men’s javelin event with a world-leading throw of 92.62 metres. The 23-year-old’s effo...
body: Sri Lanka’s Rumesh Tharanga Pathirage produced a stunning performance at the Rome Diamond League, becoming the fourth Asian athlete to breach the 90 metre mark as he won the men’s javelin event with a world-leading throw of 92.62 metres. The 23-year-old’s effort placed him second on the Asian all-time list, behind Pakistan’s Olympic champion Arshad Nadeem, whose 92.97m throw at the Paris Olympics remains the continental record. India’s Olympic gold medallist Neeraj Chopra is also among the elite Asian throwers to have crossed the 90m barrier, alongside Chinese Taipei’s Cheng Chao-Tsun.
