Pathirage’s throw set a new Sri Lankan national record, broke the Rome Diamond League meet record and moved him to eighth on the world all-time list. India’s Sachin Yadav finished eighth on his Diamond League debut with a best throw of 79.18m.

title: Praggnanandhaa defeats Gukesh, keeps title hopes alive at Norway Chess 2026
summary: India’s Praggnanandhaa Rameshbabu remained in contention for the Norway Chess 2026 title after defeating world champion Gukesh Dommaraju in Round Nine. The victory took Praggnanandhaa to 15 points, just half a point behind leader Wesley, who has 15.5 points af...
body: India’s Praggnanandhaa Rameshbabu remained in contention for the Norway Chess 2026 title after defeating world champion Gukesh Dommaraju in Round Nine. The victory took Praggnanandhaa to 15 points, just half a point behind leader Wesley, who has 15.5 points after beating Magnus Carlsen in the Armageddon tiebreak following a drawn classical game. Alireza Firouzja is third with 14.5 points after winning his Armageddon game against Vincent Keymer. With one round remaining, the title race remains wide open. In the women’s section, Bibisara Assaubayeva secured the Norway Chess Women 2026 title with a round to spare. She leads the standings with 16.5 points. Zhu Jiner moved to second place with 13 points after defeating India’s Divya Deshmukh, while Anna Muzychuk is third with 12 points.

title: India to face Pakistan in Men’s U-18 Asia Cup Hockey semifinal today
summary: India will take on arch-rivals Pakistan in the semifinal of the Men’s Under-18 Asia Cup Hockey tournament at Kakamigahara Hockey Stadium in Japan today. The match will start at 3:30 PM IST. India entered the last four after finishing second in Pool A with nine...
body: India will take on arch-rivals Pakistan in the semifinal of the Men’s Under-18 Asia Cup Hockey tournament at Kakamigahara Hockey Stadium in Japan today. The match will start at 3:30 PM IST. India entered the last four after finishing second in Pool A with nine points. The team ended the group stage with a dominant 13-1 victory over Chinese Taipei after its only defeat came against hosts Japan. Pakistan reached the semifinals with an impressive campaign built on quick counter-attacking hockey, setting up a high-profile clash between the two traditional rivals. The winner will advance to the final and face either Japan or the top team from Pool B. In the women’s U-18 Asia Cup, India progressed to the semifinals after remaining unbeaten in the group stage and will face China today at 9.30 AM IST.

title: Badminton: Arjun-Hariharan upset Malaysian pair to enter Indonesia Open Men’s Doubles QFs
summary: In Indonesia Open Badminton, the Indian pair of M. R. Arjun and Hariharan Amsakarunan has advanced to the Men’s Doubles quarterfinals in Jakarta. The Indian duo defeated the Malaysian pair of Aaron Tai and Kang Khai Xing in a hard-fought match by 16-21, 21-15,...
body: In Indonesia Open Badminton, the Indian pair of M. R. Arjun and Hariharan Amsakarunan has advanced to the Men’s Doubles quarterfinals in Jakarta. The Indian duo defeated the Malaysian pair of Aaron Tai and Kang Khai Xing in a hard-fought match by 16-21, 21-15, and 21-19 today. However, India’s challenge in the singles discipline came to an end after PV Sindhu and Ayush Shetty suffered losses in their respective pre-quarterfinals matches.

title: Indian wrestlers to begin campaign at Ulaanbaatar Open Ranking Series in Mongolia
summary: In wrestling, Indian grapplers will spearhead their campaign at the Ulaanbaatar Open Ranking Series tournament, beginning today in Mongolia. India has fielded wrestlers across men’s freestyle, women’s wrestling and Greco-Roman categories for the four-day compe...
body: In wrestling, Indian grapplers will spearhead their campaign at the Ulaanbaatar Open Ranking Series tournament, beginning today in Mongolia. India has fielded wrestlers across men’s freestyle, women’s wrestling and Greco-Roman categories for the four-day competition, the third stop of the 2026 United World Wrestling Ranking Series. Six Asian Games-qualified wrestlers will be in action, including Mansi Ahlawat in the women’s 62 kg category, Priya Malik in 76 kg, Mukul Dahiya in men’s 86 kg, and Greco-Roman wrestlers Aman, Sunil Kumar and Nitesh Kumar. In women’s wrestling, Meenakshi Goyat will compete in the 53 kg category, while former Asian champion Manisha Bhanwala will feature in the 57 kg division. Veteran Greco-Roman wrestler Sunil Kumar will lead India’s challenge in the 87 kg category.

title: India enter SAFF Women’s Championship final with 1-0 win over Bhutan
summary: In football, hosts India have booked their place in the final of the SAFF Women’s Championship after defeating Bhutan 1-0 in the semifinal at Margao, Goa, yesterday. Sanfida Nongrum scored the match-winning goal in the 58th minute to propel world number 69 Ind...
body: In football, hosts India have booked their place in the final of the SAFF Women’s Championship after defeating Bhutan 1-0 in the semifinal at Margao, Goa, yesterday. Sanfida Nongrum scored the match-winning goal in the 58th minute to propel world number 69 India into their first SAFF Women’s Championship final in six years. The five-time champions, India, will now face defending champions Bangladesh in the title clash on Saturday at 6:30 PM Indian Time. Bangladesh secured their third consecutive final appearance after defeating Nepal 2-1 in the other semifinal.

title: Virat Kohli set to miss Afghanistan ODI series due to hamstring injury
summary: India’s star batter Virat Kohli is reportedly set to miss the upcoming ODI series against Afghanistan due to injury, dealing a major setback to the hosts. Reports claimed that Kohli is suffering from a hamstring injury and will not take part in the upcoming th...
body: India’s star batter Virat Kohli is reportedly set to miss the upcoming ODI series against Afghanistan due to injury, dealing a major setback to the hosts. Reports claimed that Kohli is suffering from a hamstring injury and will not take part in the upcoming three-match contest set to begin on June 13 in Dharamsala. The Second ODI will be played in Lucknow on June 17 and the final ODI will be held on June 20 in Chennai. The injury also casts doubts over Kohli’s participation in the crucial England tour, where men in blue will play three ODIs starting from July 14. Kohli’s absence could prove to be a double blow for India, as former captain Rohit Sharma’s availability is also unclear due to an injury he suffered during IPL 2026.

title: Brazil beat Japan 2-1 to enter FIFA World Cup Round of 16
summary: In the FIFA World Cup, Brazil has entered the Round of 16 after 2-1 victory over Japan in Houston, US. Gabriel Martinelli scored the winning goal late in injury time to give five-time champion Brazil a new achievement in Round of 32 clash last night....
body: In the FIFA World Cup, Brazil has entered the Round of 16 after 2-1 victory over Japan in Houston, US. Gabriel Martinelli scored the winning goal late in injury time to give five-time champion Brazil a new achievement in Round of 32 clash last night. Martinelli, who had come on as a second-half substitute, scored in the sixth minute of stoppage time as the match appeared to be headed to extra time. Brazil will next face either the Ivory Coast or Norway on Sunday in East Rutherford, New Jersey, in the Round of 16. In another Round of 32 fixture, Paraguay defeated Germany 4-3 on spot kicks after the match finished 1-1 in extra-time to inflict Germany’s first-ever defeat in a penalty shootout in a World Cup. With this win, Paraguay advances to the World Cup Round of 16. Jose Canale scored on the first sudden death penalty kick, while goalkeeper Orlando Gill made two key saves in the shootout, Paraguay went in front in regulation, when Julio Enciso scored on a header late in the first half. Kai Havertz equalised in the 52nd minute for Germany.

title: Paraguay, Morocco and Brazil enter FIFA World Cup Round of 16
summary: Germany has been knocked out of the 2026 FIFA World Cup in the Round of 32. They were eliminated by Paraguay in a shocking 4-3 penalty shootout defeat in Boston after the match ended 1-1 following extra time. This marks Germany’s first-ever penalty shootout...
body: Germany has been knocked out of the 2026 FIFA World Cup in the Round of 32. They were eliminated by Paraguay in a shocking 4-3 penalty shootout defeat in Boston after the match ended 1-1 following extra time. This marks Germany’s first-ever penalty shootout loss in a World Cup. With this win, Paraguay advances to the World Cup Round of 16. Jose Canale scored on the first sudden death penalty kick, while goalkeeper Orlando Gill made two key saves in the shootout, Paraguay went in front in regulation, when Julio Enciso scored on a header late in the first half. Kai Havertz equalised in the 52nd minute for Germany. In another match, Morocco defeated the Netherlands 3-2 on penalties after a dramatic 1-1 draw across 120 minutes in Round of 32 clash today. Morocco’s Achraf Hakimi and Neil El Aynaoui tested Dutch goalkeeper Bart Verbruggen, while Micky van de Ven forced a world-class save from Moroccan keeper Yassine Bounou. Cody Gakpo scored a brilliant opening goal for the Netherlands after receiving a quick pass from Crysencio Summerville. After a chaotic penalty shootout featuring misses from Quinten Timber and Achraf Hakimi, Ismael Saibari scored the decisive spot-kick to send Morocco through. Earlier, Brazil has entered the Round of 16 after 2-1 victory over Japan in Houston, US. Gabriel Martinelli scored the winning goal late in injury time to give five-time champion Brazil a new achievement in Round of 32 clash last night. Martinelli, who had come on as a second-half substitute, scored in the sixth minute of stoppage time as the match appeared to be headed to extra time. Brazil will next face either the Ivory Coast or Norway on Sunday in East Rutherford, New Jersey, in the Round of 16.

title: Ayush Shetty Advances to Second Round of Indonesia Open Super 1000
summary: In badminton, India’s Ayush Shetty entered the second round of the Indonesia Open Super 1000 after defeating China’s Weng Hong Yang in a comeback victory, winning 8-21, 22-20, 21-15. India also registered a notable win in mixed doubles, with Rohan Kapoor and R...
body: In badminton, India’s Ayush Shetty entered the second round of the Indonesia Open Super 1000 after defeating China’s Weng Hong Yang in a comeback victory, winning 8-21, 22-20, 21-15. India also registered a notable win in mixed doubles, with Rohan Kapoor and Ruthvika Gadde defeating Chinese Taipei’s Yang Po-Hsuan and Hu Ling Fang in straight games, 21-14, 21-14. However, it was a disappointing day for Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty, who retired hurt from their opening-round men’s doubles match against Malaysia’s Kang Min Hyuk and Man Wei Chong after Satwik suffered an injury. In men’s singles, HS Prannoy bowed out after a hard-fought three-game loss to Ireland’s Nhat Nguyen. Meanwhile, India’s campaign in the women’s singles event will gather pace later this evening when Unnati Hooda takes on Japan’s former world champion Nozomi Okuhara.

title: England beat India by six wickets to clinch women’s T20I series
summary: In Women’s Cricket, hosts England sealed the three-match series 2-1 against India, registering a six-wicket victory in the third and final T-20 International at Taunton last night. A commanding partnership between Heather Knight and Alice Capsey helped England...
body: In Women’s Cricket, hosts England sealed the three-match series 2-1 against India, registering a six-wicket victory in the third and final T-20 International at Taunton last night. A commanding partnership between Heather Knight and Alice Capsey helped England chase down 180 with ease. Alice Capsey was declared player of the match as well as player of the series. Earlier, put into bat first, the Indian women posted 180 runs, losing 5 wickets in the stipulated 20 overs. In reply, England scored 184 for 4 in 18.3 overs.

title: India to face Bhutan in SAFF Women’s Championship semi-final in Margao today
summary: In SAFF Women’s Championship, India will face Bhutan in the second Semi-final at the Pandit Jawaharlal Nehru Stadium in Margao, Goa today. The match will kick off at 8 PM. Five-time champions India enter the knockout clash finishing top of Group B with maximum...
body: In SAFF Women’s Championship, India will face Bhutan in the second Semi-final at the Pandit Jawaharlal Nehru Stadium in Margao, Goa today. The match will kick off at 8 PM. Five-time champions India enter the knockout clash finishing top of Group B with maximum points. India began their campaign with an emphatic 11-0 victory over Maldives before convincingly blanking defending champions Bangladesh 3-0. Heading into the contest as favourites, the hosts side is further boosted with the arrival of forward Manisha Kalyan. She was released by her Peruvian club Alianza Lima for the June FIFA International Match Window that started on June 1. Ranked 69th in the FIFA rankings, India are nearly 100 places above Bhutan, who occupy the 164th position. The two nations have met only twice before, with India winning both encounters by an aggregate score of 29-0.

title: Indian Men’s Hockey Team Defeat England 3-2 in Penalty Shoot-out
summary: The Indian Men’s Hockey Team displayed remarkable defensive resilience to defeat England 3-2 in the penalty shoot-out after a hard-fought 0-0 draw in the reverse fixture of the FIH Hockey Pro League at the Lee Valley Hockey and Tennis Centre in London...
body: The Indian Men’s Hockey Team displayed remarkable defensive resilience to defeat England 3-2 in the penalty shoot-out after a hard-fought 0-0 draw in the reverse fixture of the FIH Hockey Pro League at the Lee Valley Hockey and Tennis Centre in London yesterday. Abhishek, Shilanand Lakra and Hardik Singh converted their shoot-out attempts for India in a fiercely contested encounter. The victory highlighted India’s defensive grit, composure under pressure and ability to deliver in crucial moments. Defender Sanjay, who was named Player of the Match, led a resolute defensive effort, while both goalkeepers made important contributions in preserving the clean sheet. The win marked India’s last FIH Hockey Pro League fixture before the Hockey World Cup, providing the team with a timely confidence boost ahead of the marquee tournament.

title: Ireland Defeat India by 1 Run in Second T20 International
summary: In Men’s Cricket, Ireland defeated India by 1 run in the second and final T20 International of the two-match series in Belfast last night. Hosts Ireland set a target of 155 runs for India. Put into bat, Ireland made 154 runs, losing eight wickets in the...
body: In Men’s Cricket, Ireland defeated India by 1 run in the second and final T20 International of the two-match series in Belfast last night. Hosts Ireland set a target of 155 runs for India. Put into bat, Ireland made 154 runs, losing eight wickets in the stipulated 20 overs. In reply, India could score only 153 for 9 in 20 overs. Jai Moondra was declared PLAYER OF THE MATCH and series. Ireland sensationally clinched their two-match T20 International series against India. Ireland won the first match by 34 runs.

title: India women’s cricket team qualifies for Los Angeles 2028 Olympics
summary: The Indian women’s cricket team has become one of the first sides to qualify for the Los Angeles 2028 Olympic Games. However, the men’s team will have to finish as Asia’s highest-ranked side in the ICC Men’s T20 rankings by the end of this year to secure...
body: The Indian women’s cricket team has become one of the first sides to qualify for the Los Angeles 2028 Olympic Games. However, the men’s team will have to finish as Asia’s highest-ranked side in the ICC Men’s T20 rankings by the end of this year to secure direct qualification. The International Cricket Council today confirmed that India, alongside Australia, Great Britain and South Africa, has booked its place in the women’s T20 competition at the Los Angeles 2028 Olympic Games. The direct qualification places were capped at one team from each continent. Unlike the men’s event, the women’s competition does not use ICC rankings to determine the first four qualifiers. Instead, the ongoing Women’s T20 World Cup serves as the primary qualification tournament, with the best-performing eligible team from each of the four continents earning an Olympic berth. The men’s qualification pathway follows a different route. Four direct Olympic berths will be awarded through the ICC Men’s T20 International rankings, with the highest-ranked eligible National Olympic Committee (NOC) from Africa, Asia, Europe and Oceania qualifying automatically. The rankings at the end of this year will determine the four continental representatives, meaning India must finish as Asia’s highest-ranked eligible side to secure a direct ticket to Los Angeles. Cricket will return to the Olympic Games at Los Angeles 2028 after a gap of 128 years, with six teams each competing in the men’s and women’s T20 events.

title: India’s Under-19 boys’ team enters in Asian Youth Championships semi-finals
summary: India’s Under-19 boys’ team defeated Hong Kong 3-1 in the quarterfinals of the Asian Youth Championships in Bangkok today. This win has assured team of at least a bronze medal and a direct berth in next year’s World Youth Table Tennis Championships. The...
body: India’s Under-19 boys’ team defeated Hong Kong 3-1 in the quarterfinals of the Asian Youth Championships in Bangkok today. This win has assured team of at least a bronze medal and a direct berth in next year’s World Youth Table Tennis Championships. The Indian boys will now take on top seeds China in the semi-final. However, the Indian Under-19 girls’ team fell short of the medal rounds, losing 1-3 to Hong Kong in the quarterfinals. The team will now have to qualify for next year’s World Youth Championships through the Asian qualification process.

title: Kidambi Srikanth wins silver at US Open badminton tournament
summary: In Badminton, Indian shuttler Kidambi Srikanth secured the silver medal in the men’s singles event at the US Open today after finishing as runner-up in the final. Srikanth went down against Chinese Taipei’s Su Li-yang in a hard-fought three-game contest,...
body: In Badminton, Indian shuttler Kidambi Srikanth secured the silver medal in the men’s singles event at the US Open today after finishing as runner-up in the final. Srikanth went down against Chinese Taipei’s Su Li-yang in a hard-fought three-game contest, losing 15-21, 21-16, and 9-21 in the finals. The defeat also extended Srikanth’s wait for a BWF title, as his last international title came at the French Open in 2017 during the BWF Superseries era.

title: India gets a target of 155 runs by hosts Ireland in final T20I in Belfast
summary: In Men’s Cricket, hosts Ireland set a target of 155 runs for India in the second and final T20 International of the two-match series in Belfast. In reply, India were 54 for 4 in 8 overs when reports last came in. Earlier, put into bat, Ireland posted 154...
body: In Men’s Cricket, hosts Ireland set a target of 155 runs for India in the second and final T20 International of the two-match series in Belfast. In reply, India were 54 for 4 in 8 overs when reports last came in. Earlier, put into bat, Ireland posted 154 runs, losing eight wickets in the stipulated 20 overs. Harry Tector top-scored 53 in 47 balls. For India, Prince Yadav bagged three wickets, followed by Arshdeep Singh and Shivam Dubey, two each. Harshit Rana took one wicket. Ireland lead the series 1-nil after registering a 34-run victory over India in the opening T20 International on Friday. India will be aiming to level the series, while Ireland will look to complete a clean sweep on home soil.

title: ICC Women’s T20 World Cup: India to take on Australia in their final Group A match today
summary: In the ICC Women’s T20 World Cup, India will take on Australia in their final Group A match at Lord’s Cricket Ground in London today. The match is scheduled to begin at 7:00 PM IST. Led by Harmanpreet Kaur, the Women in Blue are currently placed second in the...
body: In the ICC Women’s T20 World Cup, India will take on Australia in their final Group A match at Lord’s Cricket Ground in London today. The match is scheduled to begin at 7:00 PM IST. Led by Harmanpreet Kaur, the Women in Blue are currently placed second in the Group A standings, having won three of their four matches. Australia, meanwhile, sits comfortably at the top of the table with four wins in four games. India will be aiming for a victory over the unbeaten Australia to secure their place in the semifinals.

title: India to take on hosts Ireland in final T20I of two-match series today
summary: In Men’s cricket, India will take on hosts Ireland in the second and final T20 International of the two-match series in Belfast today. The match is scheduled to begin at 6 PM IST. Ireland leads the series 1-0 after registering a 34-run victory over India in...
body: In Men’s cricket, India will take on hosts Ireland in the second and final T20 International of the two-match series in Belfast today. The match is scheduled to begin at 6 PM IST. Ireland leads the series 1-0 after registering a 34-run victory over India in the opening T20 International on Friday. India will be aiming to level the series, while Ireland will look to complete a clean sweep on home soil.

title: Goa’s Ethan Vaz becomes India’s 96th Grandmaster
summary: Goa’s 14-year-old International Master Ethan Vaz has become India’s 96th Chess Grandmaster after securing his third and final Grandmaster norm at the Sarajevo GM Mix tournament in Bosnia and Herzegovina. Ethan won the tournament with finishing ahead of ten...
body: Goa’s 14-year-old International Master Ethan Vaz has become India’s 96th Chess Grandmaster after securing his third and final Grandmaster norm at the Sarajevo GM Mix tournament in Bosnia and Herzegovina. Ethan won the tournament with finishing ahead of ten Grandmasters. With the achievement, Ethan becomes the third Grandmaster from Goa after Anurag Mhamal and Leon Mendonca. Born on September 3, 2011, he currently has a live FIDE rating of 2522. Ethan earned his first Grandmaster norm at the GM4 Festival Saha Bijeljina in Bosnia and Herzegovina in October 2025, where he went on to win the tournament. His second norm came at the GM Mix 11 Fantastico tournament in Serbia in January this year after a final-round victory secured both the title and the norm. The young Goan has also recorded several notable performances this year. He won three medals, including two gold medals in the Rapid and Blitz events, at the Commonwealth Chess Championship. Earlier this month, he remained unbeaten at the 4th Gujarat International Open, finishing ahead of 18 of the 19 Grandmasters in the field. He also qualified for the 2026 World and Asian Youth Chess Championships after winning silver at the National Under-17 Championship. Ethan’s latest achievement adds another milestone to Goa’s growing presence in Indian chess.

title: India’s Kidambi Srikanth enters final of Men’s Singles event at US Open Badminton in California
summary: India’s Kidambi Srikanth advanced to the men’s singles final of the US Open Badminton Championships at the Titan Gym in California today. Srikanth defeated Japan’s Yudai Okimoto 22-20, 15-21 and 21-19 in a hard-fought semifinal. He will face Su Li Yang of...
body: India’s Kidambi Srikanth advanced to the men’s singles final of the US Open Badminton Championships at the Titan Gym in California today. Srikanth defeated Japan’s Yudai Okimoto 22-20, 15-21 and 21-19 in a hard-fought semifinal. He will face Su Li Yang of Chinese Taipei in the final. Earlier, the former world number one had booked his place in the last four with a 21-9, 12-21, 21-8 victory over Liao Jhuo-Fu of Chinese Taipei in the quarterfinals. Meanwhile, India’s Rounak Chouhan went down to Su Li Yang of Chinese Taipei 17-21, 19-21 in the other men’s singles semi-final. In the women’s singles, India’s Devika Sihag bowed out of the tournament after losing to Denmark’s Line Christophersen in the semifinals. Christophersen won 21-16, 11-21 and 21-11 to seal her place in the final.

title: Ireland beat India by 34 runs in first T20 International in Belfast
summary: In Men’s Cricket, Ireland defeated India by 34 runs in the first T20 International of the two-match series in Belfast last night. This is Ireland’s first victory over India in any format. Chasing 183 for victory, India were bundled out for 148 with 6 balls to...
body: In Men’s Cricket, Ireland defeated India by 34 runs in the first T20 International of the two-match series in Belfast last night. This is Ireland’s first victory over India in any format. Chasing 183 for victory, India were bundled out for 148 with 6 balls to spare. Earlier, put into bat, Ireland scored 182 for 9 in stipulated 20 overs. Matthew Hollard picked up 3 wickets by giving away 28 runs. He was adjudged player of the match. The second and final T20 International of the series will be played tomorrow.

title: France defeat Norway 4-1 to top Group I in FIFA World Cup 2026
summary: In FIFA World Cup 2026, France defeated Norway 4-1 in Massachusetts, US while Senegal beat Iraq 5-0 in Toronto in the final round of Group I games. France thumped Norway to finish top of Group I in the World Cup. Ousmane Dembele scored a first-half hat trick...
body: In FIFA World Cup 2026, France defeated Norway 4-1 in Massachusetts, US while Senegal beat Iraq 5-0 in Toronto in the final round of Group I games. France thumped Norway to finish top of Group I in the World Cup. Ousmane Dembele scored a first-half hat trick to give France a 3-1 lead over Norway. Dembele’s goals came in the seventh, 20th and 32nd minutes before Desire Doue added a late fourth. Thelonious Aasgaard scored Norway’s only goal. Norway also reached the last 32 stage with 6 points. In another Group I match, Senegal defeated Iraq 5-0. Pape Gueye scored 2 goals while Habib Diarr, Ismaila Sarr and Iliman Ndiaye scored for Senegal. This big win keeps Senegal in contention of reaching last 32 as one of best third-placed teams.

title: India thrash Pakistan 7-1 in FIH Men’s Pro League Hockey
summary: In FIH Men’s Pro League Hockey, India thrashed Pakistan 7-1 at the Lee Valley Hockey and Centre in London last night. Abu Bakar gave Pakistan the lead in the first quarter, converting a penalty corner. India had seven different goal scorers in the match....
body: In FIH Men’s Pro League Hockey, India thrashed Pakistan 7-1 at the Lee Valley Hockey and Centre in London last night. Abu Bakar gave Pakistan the lead in the first quarter, converting a penalty corner. India had seven different goal scorers in the match. Sukhjeet Singh and Harmanpreet Singh turned the tide against Pakistan with a goal each in the second quarter, before Hardik Singh, Jugraj Singh, Abhishek, and Rajkumar Pal found the back of the net to add four goals in the third quarter. Dilpreet Singh hammered the last nail in the coffin in the fourth quarter. India will now face England tomorrow.

title: India enter Badminton Asia Junior Mixed Team Championships quarterfinals in Yatsushiro, Japan
summary: India has reached the quarterfinals of the Badminton Asia Junior Mixed Team Championships with a convincing 2-0 victory over Kazakhstan in their opening Group C match in Yatsushiro, Japan. The girls’ doubles pair of Durga Esha Kandrapu and Keerthy Manchala...
body: India has reached the quarterfinals of the Badminton Asia Junior Mixed Team Championships with a convincing 2-0 victory over Kazakhstan in their opening Group C match in Yatsushiro, Japan. The girls’ doubles pair of Durga Esha Kandrapu and Keerthy Manchala gave India a strong start by defeating Alissa Kuleshova and Diana Namenova 11-5. Puneeth Suresh and Deepti Raj Aditi then edged past Mustafa Malikzhan and Alissa Kuleshova 11-10 to extend India’s lead. The Indian team went on to dominate the remaining matches and comfortably sealed the tie. India also enjoyed a one-sided second set, with none of their players conceding more than six points in any match. India is placed in Group C alongside Chinese Taipei, Kazakhstan and the Philippines. Their next match against Chinese Taipei will decide the group winner.

title: Devika Sihag, Rounak Chouhan and Kidambi Srikanth enter US Open Badminton semifinals
summary: In the US Open Badminton, India’s Devika Sihag and Rounak Chouhan have reached the semifinals in Fullerton, California. Devika caused a major upset by defeating third seed Riko Gunji of Japan in straight games, winning 22-20, 21-19 in the women’s singles...
body: In the US Open Badminton, India’s Devika Sihag and Rounak Chouhan have reached the semifinals in Fullerton, California. Devika caused a major upset by defeating third seed Riko Gunji of Japan in straight games, winning 22-20, 21-19 in the women’s singles quarterfinals. In the men’s singles, Rounak Chouhan continued his impressive run with a 23-21, 21-11 victory over Israel’s Misha Zilberman. The win took him to his first BWF World Tour Super 300 semifinal. Later, former world number one Kidambi Srikanth also booked his place in the semifinals with a 21-9, 12-21, 21-8 win over Liao Jhuo-Fu of Chinese Taipei, keeping alive the possibility of an all-India men’s singles final. In the semifinals, Chouhan will face Su Li Yang of Chinese Taipei, while Srikanth will take on Japan’s Yudai Okimoto. In the women’s singles, Devika Sihag will meet second seed, Line Christophersen.

title: India tops medals tally with seven gold at ISSF Junior World Championship in Germany
summary: Indian shooters topped the medals tally at the ISSF Junior World Championship in Germany with seven gold, finishing the campaign in style through outstanding performances in the pistol and rifle events. India finished with 24 medals – seven gold, eight silver...
body: Indian shooters topped the medals tally at the ISSF Junior World Championship in Germany with seven gold, finishing the campaign in style through outstanding performances in the pistol and rifle events. India finished with 24 medals – seven gold, eight silver and nine bronze – to top the standings, ahead of the Individual Neutral Athletes, who claimed 14 medals, including six gold, while Italy finished third with 10 medals, also featuring six gold. India’s campaign got off to a flying start as Sejal Kamble won the women’s 10m air pistol gold, while Himanshi claimed bronze. The trio of Sejal, Vanshika and Navya Bishnoi then claimed gold in the team event to give India an early lead in the championship. India continued to remain on course as Sameer won gold in the men’s 25m rapid-fire pistol event and Rohit Kanyan emerged victorious in the men’s 50m rifle 3-positions competition. Prachi Gaikwad added a silver in the women’s 50m rifle 3-positions event, while India picked up bronze medals in the men’s 50m rifle 3-positions team and men’s 25m rapid-fire pistol team events. India’s medal surge continued with Anvii Rathod claiming bronze in the women’s 10m air rifle before Pritam Kendre struck gold in the men’s 10m air rifle. India’s final medal tally could have been even more impressive had Vanshika not missed her final shot while in contention for gold and a possible junior world record and had Shambhavi not been disqualified. India also claimed bronze in the men’s and women’s 25m pistol team events. In the men’s 10m air pistol, Shiva Narwal won silver while Yug Pratap Singh Rathore took bronze to complete a double podium finish for the country. Narwal then teamed up with Sandeep Bishnoi and Chirag Sharma to secure silver in the men’s team event before partnering Vanshika Chaudhary for another silver in the 10m air pistol mixed team competition.

title: India finish atop standings with 25 medals at ISSF Junior World Championships
summary: In shooting, India finished the ISSF Junior World Championships 2026 in Suhl, Germany, with 25 medals after adding four more podium finishes in the 50m pistol events yesterday. In the men’s 50m pistol, Raj Chandra won bronze with a score of 551-8x, while...
body: In shooting, India finished the ISSF Junior World Championships 2026 in Suhl, Germany, with 25 medals after adding four more podium finishes in the 50m pistol events yesterday. In the men’s 50m pistol, Raj Chandra won bronze with a score of 551-8x, while Kumar Yogesh finished fourth with 548-4x. The Indian trio of Kumar Yogesh, Prateek and Abhinav Choudhary secured silver in the team event with a combined score of 1,610-15x. An Individual Neutral Athlete team won gold, while Ukraine claimed bronze. In the women’s 50m pistol, Aishwarya Ravichandra Balehosur narrowly missed the gold medal after scoring 540-11x. Neutral athlete Sofiia Smetankina won gold with 540-12x, while Mongolia’s Ariunzaya Amarbayasgalan took bronze. India’s Parisha Gupta finished fourth with 533-2x. However, she won bronze in the team event alongside Sanskriti Bana and Sejal Kamble. The four medals on the final day took India’s overall tally to 25, comprising seven gold, eight silver and ten bronze medals, helping the country top the standings for the second consecutive edition. India had also finished at the top of the medal standings in the previous edition in Lima, Peru, with 24 medals.

title: India beat Bangladesh by five wickets in ICC Women’s T20 World Cup
summary: In the ICC Women’s T20 World Cup, India defeated Bangladesh by five wickets in a Group A match in Manchester, England, last night. After winning the toss and electing to bat, Bangladesh posted 136 for eight in the stipulated 20 overs. For India, Radha Yadav...
body: In the ICC Women’s T20 World Cup, India defeated Bangladesh by five wickets in a Group A match in Manchester, England, last night. After winning the toss and electing to bat, Bangladesh posted 136 for eight in the stipulated 20 overs. For India, Radha Yadav picked up three wickets, while Shree Charani claimed two. In reply, India comfortably chased down the target, reaching 137 for five in 16.5 overs, riding on Shafali Verma’s 53 off 34 balls. Shafali Verma was adjudged the Player of the Match. In another Group A match, South Africa defeated the Netherlands by 88 runs in Bristol to keep their semi-final hopes alive. With this win, India have six points from three wins and one loss in Group A, trailing Australia. Australia have won all four of their matches to top the group, while South Africa, with three wins from four matches, remain strong contenders for a semi-final spot. The Harmanpreet Kaur-led Women in Blue will now need to beat Australia on Sunday to comfortably secure a place in the semi-finals.

title: Union Minister Dr. Mansukh Mandaviya stresses early awareness among athletes to promote clean sport
summary: Union Minister of Youth Affairs and Sports Dr. Mansukh Mandaviya today outlined the need to create awareness among athletes at an early stage of their sporting careers to promote clean sport and safeguard them from inadvertent doping violations. The Minister...
body: Union Minister of Youth Affairs and Sports Dr. Mansukh Mandaviya today outlined the need to create awareness among athletes at an early stage of their sporting careers to promote clean sport and safeguard them from inadvertent doping violations. The Minister said this while chairing a review meeting of the National Dope Testing Laboratory (NDTL) in New Delhi to assess its functioning, capabilities, and future roadmap. Dr. Mandaviya said that athletes participating in the National Games and Khelo India competitions will undergo mandatory anti-doping awareness sessions. The Minister also stressed the need to enhance the global footprint of NDTL. He stated that efforts should be made to increase the testing of samples from international athletes at the laboratory. The Union Minister also outlined that doping has emerged as a serious challenge for Indian sports and emphasised that awareness and education must complement enforcement measures.

title: Hockey India announces 24-member junior women’s squad for UK exposure tour
summary: Hockey India has announced the 24-member Indian Junior Women’s squad for the exposure tour of the United Kingdom from 5th to 14th of next month. Under its new coach Tim White, the team will play seven matches across Scotland and England as part of its...
body: Hockey India has announced the 24-member Indian Junior Women’s squad for the exposure tour of the United Kingdom from 5th to 14th of next month. Under its new coach Tim White, the team will play seven matches across Scotland and England as part of its preparations for upcoming international competitions, including the Junior Asia Cup. The young squad will be led by captain Khaidem Shileima Chanu, with Nidhi and Engil Harsha Rani Minz as the two goalkeepers. The defensive unit comprises Pooja Sahoo, Supriya, Madhu, F. Lalbi Aksiami, Lalneihpuii and Parwati Topno. The midfield features captain Chanu, Tanuja Toppo, Supriya Kujur, Pooja Malik, Binima Dhan, Geeta Yadav, Roshni Aind and Tanushree Dinesh Kadu. The forward line includes Sukhveer Kaur, Shashi Khasa, Lalrinpuii, Nisha Minj, Purnima Yadav, Kajal, Sanika Chandrakant Mane and Krishna Sharma. The nine-day exposure tour will begin with two matches against the Scotland Senior Women’s Team at the University of Edinburgh, followed by games against fellow junior sides from the United States, England and Belgium at the Lilleshall National Sports Centre.

title: India to face Bangladesh in Women’s T20 World Cup Group A clash today
summary: In the ICC Women’s T20 World Cup, India will take on Bangladesh in the Group A clash at the Emirates Old Trafford in Manchester today. The match is scheduled to begin at 7 PM Indian Time. Harmanpreet Kaur-led Women in Blue are currently placed second in Group...
body: In the ICC Women’s T20 World Cup, India will take on Bangladesh in the Group A clash at the Emirates Old Trafford in Manchester today. The match is scheduled to begin at 7 PM Indian Time. Harmanpreet Kaur-led Women in Blue are currently placed second in Group A, having won 2 out of 3 matches. Bangladesh have also won 2 matches, but they are placed fourth due to a lower Net Run Rate. India began their T20 World Cup campaign against arch-rivals Pakistan with a dominant 64-run victory, followed by another 95-run win over the Netherlands. However, their winning streak got broken in the last match against South Africa, where they faced a 6-wicket defeat.

title: CAB appoints Ashok Dinda as Head of Vision 2028 and fast bowling coach
summary: Cricket Association of Bengal (CAB) has named former Indian fast bowler Ashok Dinda as the Head of Vision 2028 & fast bowling coach. The CAB has also named Utpal Chatterjee as spin bowling coach under the Vision 2028 programme starting next month. Both Dinda...
body: Cricket Association of Bengal (CAB) has named former Indian fast bowler Ashok Dinda as the Head of Vision 2028 & fast bowling coach. The CAB has also named Utpal Chatterjee as spin bowling coach under the Vision 2028 programme starting next month. Both Dinda and Chatterjee have played for India. Vision 2028 is an important project for West Bengal cricket for promotion and development of players in the state. The head coaches of various men’s and women’s teams, such as Under-16, Under-19, Under-23 and Senior Bengal will also be included in the Vision along with the assistant coaches.

title: India-US open high-level trade talks in New Delhi to recalibrate proposed bilateral trade agreement
summary: India and the United States opened high-level trade talks to salvage and recalibrate a proposed bilateral trade agreement after changes in US tariff policy disrupted a framework negotiated earlier this year. Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal met...
body: India and the United States opened high-level trade talks to salvage and recalibrate a proposed bilateral trade agreement after changes in US tariff policy disrupted a framework negotiated earlier this year. Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal met United States Trade Representative Jamieson Greer and US Ambassador to India Sergio Gor in New Delhi yesterday. Both sides are aiming to conclude an interim trade pact before 24th July, when Washington’s temporary 10% tariff on imports from trading partners is set to expire. On February 20, the US Supreme Court struck down sweeping tariffs. This led the US administration to impose a temporary 10% tariff under Section 122 of the Trade Act on all countries for 150 days starting February 24, which is due to expire on July 24. Following a de-escalation in bilateral trade tensions, the U.S. reduced the reciprocal tariff on most Indian goods to 18% and removed a previous 25% penalty tariff. India had also proposed reducing or eliminating tariffs on a range of US industrial and agricultural goods, including dried distillers’ grains, red sorghum for animal feed, tree nuts, fruits, soybean oil, wine and spirits, among others. Earlier, Piyush Goyal said in a social media post that he was looking forward to productive discussions on the India-US bilateral trade agreement. Meanwhile, Sergio Gor also shared a message on social media. He said India and the US are moving decisively towards finalising a strong bilateral trade agreement that will unlock new economic opportunities for both countries and significantly deepen the US-India economic partnership.

title: Hockey India announces 24-member junior women’s squad for upcoming UK tour beginning next month
summary: Hockey India has announced the 24-member junior women’s squad for the exposure tour of the United Kingdom, scheduled from July 5 to 14. Under its new coach Tim White, the team will play seven matches across Scotland and England as part of its preparations for...
body: Hockey India has announced the 24-member junior women’s squad for the exposure tour of the United Kingdom, scheduled from July 5 to 14. Under its new coach Tim White, the team will play seven matches across Scotland and England as part of its preparations for upcoming international competitions, including the Junior Asia Cup. The young squad will be led by captain Khaidem Shileima Chanu, with Nidhi and Engil Harsha Rani Minz as the two goalkeepers. The defensive unit comprises Pooja Sahoo, Supriya, Madhu, F. Lalbi Aksiami, Lalneihpuii and Parwati Topno. The midfield features captain Chanu, Tanuja Toppo, Supriya Kujur, Pooja Malik, Binima Dhan, Geeta Yadav, Roshni Aind and Tanushree Dinesh Kadu. The nine-day exposure tour will begin with two matches against the Scotland Senior Women’s Team at the University of Edinburgh, followed by games against fellow junior sides from the United States, England and Belgium at the Lilleshall National Sports Centre.

title: Hockey: India defeats Pakistan 4-3 in thrilling FIH Men’s Pro League 2025-26 clash in London
summary: In Hockey, India defeated Pakistan 4-3 in the thrilling FIH Men’s Pro League 2025-26 clash in London this evening. Pakistan took a shock lead against the run of play in the first quarter, as Ahmad Nadeem scored after a penalty corner. After squandering a...
body: In Hockey, India defeated Pakistan 4-3 in the thrilling FIH Men’s Pro League 2025-26 clash in London this evening. Pakistan took a shock lead against the run of play in the first quarter, as Ahmad Nadeem scored after a penalty corner. After squandering a number of penalty corners, India finally equalized in the second quarter, courtesy of Abhishek Nain’s goal. Nilakanta Sharma scored soon after to give India the lead. Sukhjeet Singh added India’s third goal with a superb strike. Rajinder Singh sealed the win with a brilliant dragflick from a penalty corner in the fourth quarter. Shakeel Moin and Abu Mahmood scored the other two goals for Pakistan. The India-Pakistan encounters are the most anticipated fixtures of the leg, reigniting one of hockey’s most storied rivalries on the international stage. The Indian team was high on confidence, having won two of their last four matches at the Rotterdam leg. India defeated reigning Olympic and Pro League champions the Netherlands in Rotterdam last week. The Indian team also beat world No. 1 Germany during the Rotterdam leg. During the London leg, India will face England on Thursday and then take on Pakistan once again on 26th of June before concluding the tour with a second encounter against England on 28th of June. Men in Blue entered the London leg placed eighth in the FIH Pro League 2025-26 standings with 10 points from 12 matcheswhile Pakistan is at the bottom with zero points.

title: FIH Pro League: Indian men’s hockey team to face arch-rivals Pakistan in opening match
summary: In Hockey, the Indian men’s hockey team will take on arch-rivals Pakistan in the opening match of the FIH Pro League today. This will be the first encounter between India and Pakistan since their Asian Champions Trophy clash in Hulunbuir, China, in 2024,...
body: In Hockey, the Indian men’s hockey team will take on arch-rivals Pakistan in the opening match of the FIH Pro League today. This will be the first encounter between India and Pakistan since their Asian Champions Trophy clash in Hulunbuir, China, in 2024, where India emerged victorious with a 2-1 win. The head-to-head record over the last 10 years heavily favours India, who remain undefeated across 17 matches, with 15 wins and two draws. The Indian hockey team will aim to carry forward their momentum after a 3-2 win over world number two the Netherlands yesterday in their final match of the Rotterdam leg of the FIH Pro League. Jugraj Singh, Abhishek, and Rajinder Singh scored in this impressive victory. India’s other win in the Rotterdam round of the tournament was against reigning world champions and world number five Germany.

title: Lakshya Sen and Tanvi Sharma to lead India’s challenge at US Open Super Badminton Tournament in California
summary: Star shuttlers Lakshya Sen and Tanvi Sharma will lead India’s challenge at the US Open Super Badminton Tournament, beginning today in California. World No. 14 Lakshya Sen, seeded second, will take on Belgium’s Julien Carraggi in the opening round of the men’s...
body: Star shuttlers Lakshya Sen and Tanvi Sharma will lead India’s challenge at the US Open Super Badminton Tournament, beginning today in California. World No. 14 Lakshya Sen, seeded second, will take on Belgium’s Julien Carraggi in the opening round of the men’s singles event. Former World Championships silver medallist Kidambi Srikanth, seeded fifth, will face Chinese Taipei’s Liao Jhuo-Fu in his first-round match. Unseeded Indian shuttler Saneeth Dayanand will begin his campaign against Japan’s fourth seed Yudai Okimoto, while S. Sankar Muthusamy Subramanian will take on a qualifier in the round of 32. In women’s singles, India has a strong contingent of seven players in the main draw, led by fifth seed Tanvi Sharma and sixth seed Devika Sihag. Seventeen-year-old Tanvi will open her campaign against Germany’s Yvonne Li. In women’s doubles, Commonwealth Games bronze medallists Treesa Jolly and Gayatri Gopichand will face Spain’s Paula Lopez and Lucia Rodriguez in the opening round. In mixed doubles, seventh seeds Dhruv Rawat and Maneesha K will begin their campaign against Indonesia’s Wirawan Ihsan Adam and Serena Kani.

title: FIFA World Cup continues with crucial group-stage encounters as teams battle for place in knockout rounds
summary: The FIFA World Cup continues with crucial group-stage encounters today as teams battle for a place in the knockout rounds. In Group K, Portugal will face Uzbekistan at Houston in US with Cristiano Ronaldo’s side aiming to bounce back after being held to a...
body: The FIFA World Cup continues with crucial group-stage encounters today as teams battle for a place in the knockout rounds. In Group K, Portugal will face Uzbekistan at Houston in US with Cristiano Ronaldo’s side aiming to bounce back after being held to a draw in their opening match. The match will begin at 10:30 PM. In Group L, England meet Ghana in a highly anticipated clash in Boston, US at 1:30 am. England will be looking to build on their impressive start to the tournament, while Ghana seek another positive result to boost their qualification hopes. In another Group L fixture, Croatia will take on Panama at Toronto in Canada from 4:30 am. Croatia need a strong response after their opening defeat, while Panama are searching for their first win of the campaign. Later, Colombia lock horns with DR Congo in Guadalajara from 7:30 am tomorrow in Group K fixture. Meanwhile, Argentina, France and Norway have confirmed their progression to the knockout rounds. Argentina registered a convincing 2-nil win over Austria in a Group J fixture at Arlington, US last night. Captain Lionel Messi starred for the South American giants, scoring both goals. With the brace, Messi took his World Cup tally to 18 goals, surpassing Germany legend Miroslav Klose’s record of 16 to become the highest goal-scorer in FIFA World Cup history. In another match, Norway edged past Senegal 3-2 in their Group I clash at the New York Stadium to secured their place in the Round of 32. In another key result, France cruised into the Knockout rounds with a dominant 3-nil victory over Iraq in a Group I match at the Philadelphia Stadium early this morning. Kylian Mbappé scored twice, while Ousmane Dembélé added the third as the French side secured qualification in style. Besides, Algeria registered a 2-1 victory over debutants Jordan in a Group J encounter at Santa Clara in the United States today, keeping their hopes alive of progressing to the knockout stages. With the race for the Round of 32 intensifying, today’s results could prove decisive in shaping the knockout-stage picture.

title: FIFA World Cup: Algeria beat Jordan 2-1; Norway, Argentina and France secure Round of 32
summary: In the FIFA World Cup, Algeria thrashed debutants Jordan by 2-1 in a Group J encounter in Santa Clara, USA today. In another match, Norway defeated Senegal 3-2 in their Group I clash today at the New York Stadium and sealed their progress into the...
body: In the FIFA World Cup, Algeria thrashed debutants Jordan by 2-1 in a Group J encounter in Santa Clara, USA today. In another match, Norway defeated Senegal 3-2 in their Group I clash today at the New York Stadium and sealed their progress into the round-of-32. In another super clash, Argentina defeated Austria 2-0 in their Group J match in Arlington, United States, last night to secure a place in the round of 32. Argentina captain Lionel Messi took charge and scored two goals. With his two goals, Messi took his FIFA World Cup goal tally to 18, surpassing Germany’s Miroslav Klose’s record of 16 goals to become the highest goal-scorer in World Cup history. France defeated Iraq 3-0 in a Group I match at the Philadelphia Stadium early morning to secure their passage to the knockout stages. Kylian Mbappé scored twice, and Ousmane Dembélé added a third goal.

title: ICC Women’s T20 World Cup: South Africa beat India by 6 wickets in Group A match
summary: In Women’s Cricket, riding on an unbeaten 81 runs by Marizanne Kapp, South Africa defeated India by 6 wickets in a group A match of ICC T20 World Cup in Manchester last night. Chasing a target of 159 runs set by India, South Africa scored 161 for four with 5...
body: In Women’s Cricket, riding on an unbeaten 81 runs by Marizanne Kapp, South Africa defeated India by 6 wickets in a group A match of ICC T20 World Cup in Manchester last night. Chasing a target of 159 runs set by India, South Africa scored 161 for four with 5 balls to spare. Tazmin Brits made 40 runs. For India Shree Charani scalped 3 wickets. Earlier, India won the toss and elected to bat first. India scored 158 runs with the loss of seven wickets in their stipulated 20 overs. Shafali Verma was the highest scorer for India with 31 runs. For South Africa, Marizanne Kapp and Shabnim Ismail took two wickets each. Marizanne Kapp was adjudged as the Player of the Match.This match marked a major milestone for captain Harmanpreet Kaur, who became the first cricketer male or female to play 200 T20 internationals.

title: FIFA World Cup 2026: Cape Verde hold Uruguay to 2-2 draw in Group H clash
summary: In FIFA World Cup 2026, Cape Verde held Uruguay to a 2-2 draw in their Group H match in Florida today. Cape Verde took the lead in the 21st minute through Kevin Pina, who scored a stunning direct free kick and registered his country’s first-ever World Cup...
body: In FIFA World Cup 2026, Cape Verde held Uruguay to a 2-2 draw in their Group H match in Florida today. Cape Verde took the lead in the 21st minute through Kevin Pina, who scored a stunning direct free kick and registered his country’s first-ever World Cup goal. Uruguay responded with two goals from Maxi Araújo and Agustín Canobbio to go ahead. However, Cape Verde equalised through Hélder Varela, who took advantage of a defensive error to make it 2-2. The result leaves both teams on two points going into the final round of Group H matches. Earlier, Iran and Belgium played out a goalless draw in their Group G clash in California last night. Belgium were reduced to 10 men in the 66th minute after Nathan Ngoy was sent off for fouling Mehdi Taremi, but Iran failed to capitalise. The result leaves both teams with two draws from two matches. Earlier, Spain booked their place in the next round with a commanding 4-0 victory over Saudi Arabia in Group H clash in Atlanta. After a goalless draw against Cape Verde in their opener, Spain looked much sharper with Lamine Yamal back in the starting lineup. The teenager opened the scoring in the 10th minute from a move created by Dani Olmo. Mikel Oyarzabal then scored twice in quick succession before an own goal by Hassan Altambakti completed the scoring. Spain now has four points from two matches, while Saudi Arabia remains on one point. In today’s fixtures, New Zealand will face Egypt in Group G clash at 6:30 AM IST, while Argentina will take on Austria at in Group J match at 10:30 PM IST.

title: Egypt register first-ever FIFA World Cup victory, beat New Zealand 3-1
summary: In FIFA World Cup 2026, Egypt registered their first-ever World Cup victory, 3-1, over New Zealand in Vancouver this morning. Liverpool star Mohamed Salah scored his first goal of the tournament. With this win, Egypt took over first place in Group G. Earlier,...
body: In FIFA World Cup 2026, Egypt registered their first-ever World Cup victory, 3-1, over New Zealand in Vancouver this morning. Liverpool star Mohamed Salah scored his first goal of the tournament. With this win, Egypt took over first place in Group G. Earlier, Cape Verde held Uruguay to a 2-2 draw in their Group H match in Florida today. Iran and Belgium also played out a goalless draw in their Group G clash in California last night. Belgium were reduced to 10 men in the 66th minute after Nathan Ngoy was sent off for fouling Mehdi Taremi, but Iran failed to capitalise. Previously, Spain booked their place in the next round with a commanding 4-nil victory over Saudi Arabia in Group H clash in Atlanta. Spain now has four points from two matches, while Saudi Arabia remains on one point. In today’s fixtures, Argentina will take on Austria at in Group J. The match will begin at 10:30 PM IST.

title: Bengaluru to host 7th Air Force Arjan Singh Memorial Hockey Tournament 2026 from tomorrow
summary: Bengaluru will host the 7th edition of the Air Force Arjan Singh Memorial Hockey Tournament 2026 at the Air Marshal Amit Singh Bakshi Hockey Stadium, Air Force Station Jalahalli, from tomorrow till the 3rd of next month. Addressing a press conference in...
body: Bengaluru will host the 7th edition of the Air Force Arjan Singh Memorial Hockey Tournament 2026 at the Air Marshal Amit Singh Bakshi Hockey Stadium, Air Force Station Jalahalli, from tomorrow till the 3rd of next month. Addressing a press conference in Bengaluru, Air Marshal S. Sivakumar said that 16 teams from across the country will participate in the prestigious tournament. Teams representing the Army, Railways, CISF, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu and several other states and organisations will compete for the title. Air Marshal Sivakumar said the tournament is being held in Bengaluru for the first time as part of an initiative to take the event to different parts of the country and promote hockey among a wider audience. He added that no foreign teams are participating in this year’s edition due to the ongoing crisis in the Middle East. The tournament will feature a substantially enhanced prize pool, with the total prize money increased from seven lakh 51 thousand rupees to 26 lakh 25 thousand rupees. The winning team will receive a cash award of 10 lakh rupees, while the runners-up will be awarded eight lakh rupees. The tournament is expected to witness keen competition among some of the country’s leading hockey teams.

title: BCCI announces India’s ODI squad for England tour under captain Shubman Gill
summary: The Board of Control for Cricket in India (BCCI) has announced India’s 15-member ODI squad for the upcoming England tour starting on July 14. The squad includes return of senior players like Virat Kohli and Jasprit Bumrah under the captaincy of Shubman Gill...
body: The Board of Control for Cricket in India (BCCI) has announced India’s 15-member ODI squad for the upcoming England tour starting on July 14. The squad includes return of senior players like Virat Kohli and Jasprit Bumrah under the captaincy of Shubman Gill with Shreyas Iyer as vice-captain. The BCCI has also included Rohit Sharma, KL Rahul, Ishan Kishan, Washington Sundar, Axar Patel, Nitish Kumar Reddy, Kuldeep Yadav, Jasprit Bumrah, Prasidh Krishna, Harshit Rana, Arshdeep Singh, and Gurnoor Brar. The three-match ODI series will begin on July 14 at Edgbaston, followed by the second match on July 16 in Cardiff, and the final ODI on July 19 at the Iconic Lord’s. In another update, Prasidh Krishna has been added to India’s T20I squad for the two-match series against Ireland after Varun Chakaravarthy was ruled out due to a foot injury sustained during IPL 2026. India will first play the T20I series against Ireland on June 26 and 28 in Belfast, before moving on to a five-match T20I series against England, followed by the ODI series.

title: FIH Pro League: India beat Netherlands 3-2 in their final match in Rotterdam
summary: In Men’s Hockey, India defeated Netherlands 3-2 in their final FIH Pro League match in Rotterdam yesterday. Jugraj Singh, Abhishek, and Rajinder Singh scored the goals, helping India register their second win of the leg against the Dutch side. The Netherlands...
body: In Men’s Hockey, India defeated Netherlands 3-2 in their final FIH Pro League match in Rotterdam yesterday. Jugraj Singh, Abhishek, and Rajinder Singh scored the goals, helping India register their second win of the leg against the Dutch side. The Netherlands started strongly and created early pressure, but India’s defence settled quickly. After a goalless first quarter, INDIA took the lead with a penalty corner goal through Jugraj Singh in the 18th minute. The Dutch equalised soon after, but Abhishek restored India’s lead before halftime to make it 2-1. The final quarter saw the Netherlands pushing hard for an equaliser, but errors in the penalty corner setup continued to frustrate the hosts. In the second half, Rajinder Singh extended India’s advantage in the 56th minute from a penalty corner. The Netherlands men’s national field hockey team responded late through Koen Bijen, but India held firm in the closing stages to seal the win. Stand-in captain Hardik Singh was named Player of the Match for his leadership and strong midfield performance. India’s defence stood out in the final quarter, especially while defending multiple penalty corners, to secure a valuable victory.

title: India adds four medals at ISSF Junior World Championship
summary: In shooting, India added four more medals at the ISSF Junior World Championship, including a gold medal by teenager Pritam Kendre, to strengthen its position at the top of the medals tally. Pritam Kendre clinched gold in the 10-metre Air Rifle Men’s Junior...
body: In shooting, India added four more medals at the ISSF Junior World Championship, including a gold medal by teenager Pritam Kendre, to strengthen its position at the top of the medals tally. Pritam Kendre clinched gold in the 10-metre Air Rifle Men’s Junior event, earning country’s fifth gold medal of the championship. In the 25-metre Pistol Women Junior Team event, the trio of Anjali Mahendra Bhagwat, Parisha Gupta and Nithila Ivy Darling Praveen Christopher secured the bronze medal. Another bronze came in the 25-metre Pistol Men Junior Team event, where Abhinav Choudhary, Raj Chandra and Jatin delivered a strong performance to finish among the medal winners. Earlier, Anvii Rathod won bronze in the 10-metre Air Rifle Women Junior event. India’s overall medal tally has now risen to 12, comprising five gold, one silver and six bronze medals.

title: India Win Women’s 4x100m Relay Gold at Asian Relays Championships
summary: At the Asian Relays Championships, India clinched the gold medal in the women’s 4x100m relay on the final day of the second edition of the tournament at Shaoxing in China. The Indian quartet of Srabani Nanda, SS Sneha, Sudeshna Shivankar and Tamanna clocked...
body: At the Asian Relays Championships, India clinched the gold medal in the women’s 4x100m relay on the final day of the second edition of the tournament at Shaoxing in China. The Indian quartet of Srabani Nanda, SS Sneha, Sudeshna Shivankar and Tamanna clocked 43.85 seconds, a season best. Indian mixed 4x400m relay team added silver to the medal tally with Theerthesh Shetty, Poovamma MR, Bharath Sridhar and Neeru Pathak clocking 3:17.06 seconds. Earlier, the Indian mixed 4x100m team bagged bronze. The members of the Indian team were Pranav Gaurav, Tamanna, Animesh Kujur and SS Sneha.

title: India A to take on Sri Lanka A in tri-series final in Dambulla this morning
summary: India A will take on Sri Lanka A in the tri-series cricket final, scheduled to be played in Dambulla today. The match will start at 10 AM Indian time. Smarting from an ill-tempered defeat in their previous match, India A will look to settle scores when they...
body: India A will take on Sri Lanka A in the tri-series cricket final, scheduled to be played in Dambulla today. The match will start at 10 AM Indian time. Smarting from an ill-tempered defeat in their previous match, India A will look to settle scores when they take on Sri Lanka A in the final, with teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi set to be in the spotlight once again. In their first match of the tournament, India A managed a narrow escape against the hosts Sri Lanka A. In the second game, they were battered by Afghanistan A in a rain-curtailed contest. Then, in their third game against Sri Lanka A, the India A team lost an ill-tempered Super Over clash to the hosts. Just when their chances of qualification looked really slim, India A produced a terrific performance in their final round-robin game against Afghanistan A. The margin of victory was so emphatic that from the brink of elimination, India A became the first team to qualify for the final, leaving Afghanistan and Sri Lanka battling for the remaining spot.

title: PM Modi Congratulates Indian Women’s Hockey Team on Winning FIH Women’s Nations Cup
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi today congratulated the Indian Women’s Hockey team for winning the FIH Hockey Women’s Nations Cup. In a social media post, the Prime Minister praised the team for their exceptional performance throughout the tournament. He said...
body: Prime Minister Narendra Modi today congratulated the Indian Women’s Hockey team for winning the FIH Hockey Women’s Nations Cup. In a social media post, the Prime Minister praised the team for their exceptional performance throughout the tournament. He said the victory will inspire many young players to take up hockey. The Prime Minister also extended his best wishes to the team for future success.

title: India A Beat Sri Lanka A by 66 Runs, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi Smashes 94 off 29 Balls
summary: In Cricket, India A defeated Hosts Sri Lanka by 66 runs in the Summit Clash of Tri-Nation A Series in Dambulla today. Put into bat, India A posted a mammoth 377 runs for the loss of nine wickets in stipulated 50 overs. Teenage batting sensation Vaibhav...
body: In Cricket, India A defeated Hosts Sri Lanka by 66 runs in the Summit Clash of Tri-Nation A Series in Dambulla today. Put into bat, India A posted a mammoth 377 runs for the loss of nine wickets in stipulated 50 overs. Teenage batting sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi continued his meteoric rise in world cricket with a breathtaking innings of 94 runs off just 29 balls, including record-breaking half-century in just 11 balls. Tilak Varma also made valuable contribution with 67 runs off 90 balls. For Sri Lanka A, Kugathas Mathulan, Ravindu Fernando and Wanuja Sahan claimed two wickets each. In reply, Sri Lanka were bowled out for 311 in 47.1 overs. For Hosts, Wanuja Sahhan was the top-scorer with 62 runs off 69-balls followed by Sadeera Samarawickrama with 52 runs off 44 runs. For visitors, Yash Thakur and Vipraj Nigam picked three wickets each and Ankul Roy took two wickets.

title: India Finish with 12 Medals, Including Eight Golds, at Grand Prix Usti nad Labem
summary: India’s Commonwealth Games-bound boxers finished with a rich haul of 12 medals, including eight golds, capping off a commanding display at the invitational 56th Grand Prix Usti nad Labem in Czechia. The five-member women’s team led the charge by winning four...
body: India’s Commonwealth Games-bound boxers finished with a rich haul of 12 medals, including eight golds, capping off a commanding display at the invitational 56th Grand Prix Usti nad Labem in Czechia. The five-member women’s team led the charge by winning four gold medals and one silver. Olympic medallist Lovlina Borgohain in 75 kilogram, reigning Asian champion Preeti Pawar in 54 kilogram, Sakshi Chaudhary in 51 kilogram and Priya Ghanghas in 60 kilogram all struck gold by registering unanimous victories in their respective finals. Parveen Hooda in 65 kilogram added a silver medal to complete an impressive showing by the women’s squad. The Men’s team matched the women’s success with four gold medals. Jadumani Singh 55 kilogram, Sachin 60 kilogram, Ankush 80kilogram and Narender in plus 90 kilogram emerged champions in their respective categories. Lovlina defeated Italy’s Melissa Gemini 5-Nil in the final, while Preeti outclassed France’s Jeyssa Marcel by a similar margin. Sakshi beat France’s Mayssoun Bourega 5-Nil and Priya secured a dominant win over home favourite Barbora Maxova of Czechia. Jadumani defeated England’s Abdul Burton 4-1 in the final, while Sachin and Ankush registered comprehensive 5-Nil victories over Hungary’s Veres Rohland and Czech Republic’s Florian Daniel. Narender edged past Armenia’s Davit Chaloyan 3-2 in a closely fought heavyweight final. Aditya Pratap Yadav in 65 kilogram and Kapil Pokhariya in 90 kilogram settled for silver medals after narrow defeats in their title bouts, while Sumit Kundu in 70 kilogram claimed bronze.

title: India Beat New Zealand 2-0 to Clinch Second FIH Women’s Nations Cup Title
summary: In Hockey, India defeated hosts New Zealand 2-0 in the final in Auckland today to win their second FIH Women’s Nations Cup title and secured promotion to the FIH Women’s Pro League. Navneet Kaur gave India the early lead before Sunelita Toppo doubled India’s...
body: In Hockey, India defeated hosts New Zealand 2-0 in the final in Auckland today to win their second FIH Women’s Nations Cup title and secured promotion to the FIH Women’s Pro League. Navneet Kaur gave India the early lead before Sunelita Toppo doubled India’s advantage. The team produced a stellar defensive display to protect their lead and win the title, which also marked their return to the Pro League next season. Lalremsiami won the ‘Player of the Match’ award in the final, while Deepika finished as the joint-top scorer in the tournament with six goals to her name – sharing the honours with USA’s Ashley Sessa. This is India’s second Nations Cup title, having won the inaugural edition of the tournament in 2022. India remained unbeaten throughout their campaign, defeating USA, 3-2; Japan 2-1; and Uruguay 3-2 in the Pool A matches, before registering a commanding 6-0 win over Chile in the semi-final. To recognise the teams’ achievements, Hockey India announced a cash award of 3 lakh rupees for each player and 1.5 lakh rupees for each member of the support staff.

title: FIFA World Cup: Saudi Arabia to Face Spain in Group H Clash Tonight
summary: In FIFA World Cup, Spain will take on Saudi Arabia in Group H fixture tonight at Atlanta in United States. The match will begin at 9:30 PM Indian Time. In another clash, Belgium will face Iran in Group G match at Los Angeles in US at 12:30 AM. In the other...
body: In FIFA World Cup, Spain will take on Saudi Arabia in Group H fixture tonight at Atlanta in United States. The match will begin at 9:30 PM Indian Time. In another clash, Belgium will face Iran in Group G match at Los Angeles in US at 12:30 AM. In the other matches, Germany secured berth in the knockout stage after defeating Ivory Coast 2-1 in Toronto, Canada. The four-time champions bounced back strongly after group-stage exits in 2018 and 2022. A dominant Japan produced the biggest margin of victory for an Asian nation in a World Cup match as they eliminated Tunisia with a 4-0 win. A sensational performance from goalkeeper Eloy Room created history for Curacao as they earned a first World Cup point by holding Ecuador to a 0-0 draw in Kansas City.

title: Javelin Throw: India’s Neeraj Chopra finishes fourth at 2026 Doha Diamond League
summary: Star Indian javelin thrower Neeraj Chopra finished fourth at the Doha Diamond League last night in his first competition of the season. The 28-year-old recorded a best throw of 85.69 metres in his third attempt. It was his first event since recovering from a...
body: Star Indian javelin thrower Neeraj Chopra finished fourth at the Doha Diamond League last night in his first competition of the season. The 28-year-old recorded a best throw of 85.69 metres in his third attempt. It was his first event since recovering from a back injury that delayed the start of his season. Sri Lanka’s Rumesh Tharanga Pathirage won the event with a throw of 88.68 metres. Two-time world champion Andersen Peters of Grenada finished second with 86.38 metres, while American Curtis Thompson took third place with 85.99 metres. Despite missing out on a podium finish, Chopra crossed the qualifying mark of 82.61 metres set by the Athletics Federation of India for the upcoming Commonwealth Games. Chopra has already been named in India’s 32-member squad for the Commonwealth Games, which will be held in Glasgow from July 23 to August 2.

title: India add five more medals at ISSF Junior World Championship in Germany
summary: In shooting, India added five more medals to its tally on the second day of the ISSF Junior World Championship 2026 in Suhl, Germany, taking its overall medal count to eight. Sameer won the gold medal in the 25-metre Rapid Fire Pistol Men’s Junior event,...
body: In shooting, India added five more medals to its tally on the second day of the ISSF Junior World Championship 2026 in Suhl, Germany, taking its overall medal count to eight. Sameer won the gold medal in the 25-metre Rapid Fire Pistol Men’s Junior event, producing a composed display in the final to finish with 28 hits. Rohit Kanyan also struck gold in the 50-metre Rifle Three Positions Men’s Junior event with an outstanding performance. Prachi Gaikwad secured the silver medal in the 50-metre Rifle Three Positions Women’s Junior event, finishing second with a score of 354.7. India won a bronze medal in the 50-metre Rifle Three Positions Men’s Junior Team event through Rohit Kanyan, Vedant Nitin Waghmare and Hithesh Srinivasan. Another bronze came in the 25-metre Rapid Fire Pistol Men’s Junior Team event, where Sameer, Abhinav Choudhary and Sahil Choudhary earned a podium finish.

title: Men’s Cricket: India to take on Afghanistan in 3rd and final ODI of series in Chennai this afternoon
summary: In Cricket, the third and final ODI between India and Afghanistan will be played at the MA Chidambaram Stadium in Chennai this afternoon. The match is scheduled to begin at 1:30 PM. The hosts have already secured the series with a 2-nil lead and will be...
body: In Cricket, the third and final ODI between India and Afghanistan will be played at the MA Chidambaram Stadium in Chennai this afternoon. The match is scheduled to begin at 1:30 PM. The hosts have already secured the series with a 2-nil lead and will be aiming to complete a clean sweep of the three-match series. Meanwhile, fast bowler Harshit Rana has joined the Indian squad for the final ODI after recovering from a knee injury. The Shubhman Gill-led side won the opening ODI by seven wickets in Dharamshala. The rain-affected match was reduced to 25 overs per side. India then claimed a comfortable 170-run victory in the second ODI in Lucknow, with centuries from captain Shubman Gill and Ishan Kishan. Earlier in the tour, India also defeated Afghanistan in the one-off Test match by an innings and 300 runs inside three days.

title: India sweep ODI series against Afghanistan with nine-wicket win
summary: In Cricket, India completed a clean sweep of the three-match one-day international series with a comprehensive nine-wicket win over Afghanistan in the final match at the MA Chidambaram Stadium in Chennai today, as Yashasvi Jaiswal smashed an unbeaten century....
body: In Cricket, India completed a clean sweep of the three-match one-day international series with a comprehensive nine-wicket win over Afghanistan in the final match at the MA Chidambaram Stadium in Chennai today, as Yashasvi Jaiswal smashed an unbeaten century. Chasing a modest 219-run target, India raced to victory in just 28.4 overs, losing one wicket. Jaiswal slammed an unbeaten 110 off 86 balls, while Rohit scored 79 off 69 deliveries. Earlier, pacer Prasidh Krishna ripped through Afghanistan’s top order, claiming four early wickets to finish with outstanding figures as the visitors were bowled out for 218 in 44.2 overs. However, despite Krishna’s destructive spell, Afghanistan skipper Hashmatullah Shahidi stood firm with his maiden ODI century. The hosts have already secured the series with a 2-nil lead.

title: 22 rowers inducted into TOPS Development Group ahead of Asian Games 2026
summary: 22 rowers have been inducted into the Target Olympic Podium Scheme (TOPS) Development Group ahead of the 2026 Asian Games. Sports Ministry said that the induction of these rowers is another effort to build sustainable bench strength across several boat categor...
body: 22 rowers have been inducted into the Target Olympic Podium Scheme (TOPS) Development Group ahead of the 2026 Asian Games. Sports Ministry said that the induction of these rowers is another effort to build sustainable bench strength across several boat categories rather than focusing only on individual medal prospects. The crew will now prepare for major international competitions featuring Rowing, including the Asian Games and World Cup circuit. Among those inducted are Paris 2024 Olympian Balraj Panwar, Asian Games 2022 silver medalist Arvind Singh, and Asian Games 2022 double medalist Jaswinder Singh. While the majority of the inducted athletes are being prepared with a direct focus on the Asian Games 2026 cycle, some crews are also part of India’s broader international development pathway targeting future competitions, like the Commonwealth Games 2030 set to be hosted in the country.

title: India to face England in third and final T20 International at Taunton
summary: In Women’s Cricket, India to take on England in the third and final Twenty20 International at the Cooper Associates County Ground, Taunton, England. The match will begin at 11:00 PM, India Standard Time. Earlier, India won the first match of the series by 38 r...
body: In Women’s Cricket, India to take on England in the third and final Twenty20 International at the Cooper Associates County Ground, Taunton, England. The match will begin at 11:00 PM, India Standard Time. Earlier, India won the first match of the series by 38 runs in Chelmsford thanks to half-centuries by Yastika Bhatia and Jemimah Rodrigues, before Freya Kemp’s all-round showing helped England level the series with a 26-run victory in Bristol.

title: India assured of six medals at World Boxing Cup 2026 after strong quarterfinal performance
summary: Indian boxers continued their impressive run at the World Boxing Cup 2026 in Guiyang, China, assuring the country of six medals after a strong quarterfinal performance. In the women’s events, Prachi advanced to the semifinals with a 4-1 win over Chinese...
body: Indian boxers continued their impressive run at the World Boxing Cup 2026 in Guiyang, China, assuring the country of six medals after a strong quarterfinal performance. In the women’s events, Prachi advanced to the semifinals with a 4-1 win over Chinese Taipei’s Shih Yi Wu In 57 kg category, while in 51kg bout Minakshi defeated Kazakhstan’s Alua Balkibekova 5-0. Meanwhile, In 48 kg category Jyoti and Jugnoo in 85kg division had already assured India of podium finishes after advancing to the semi-finals. In Mens category, Deepak outclassed Azerbaijan’s Nabi Isgandarov with a unanimous 5-0 decision in 70 kg bout, While In 55kg category Nikhil also impressed with a dominant 5-0 win over Azerbaijan’s Amin Mammadzada to progress to the semifinals.

title: Macau Open: Ashmita Chaliha enters semifinals with straight-game victory over Kim Min Sun
summary: In Badminton, India’s Ashmita Chaliha stormed into the women’s singles semifinals of the Macau Open in China today. Ashmita secured her place in the last four with a straight-game victory over South Korea’s Kim Min Sun 21-16, 21-18 in the quarterfinals. She...
body: In Badminton, India’s Ashmita Chaliha stormed into the women’s singles semifinals of the Macau Open in China today. Ashmita secured her place in the last four with a straight-game victory over South Korea’s Kim Min Sun 21-16, 21-18 in the quarterfinals. She will next face South Korea’s Park Ga-eun in the semifinals tomorrow. Earlier, Ashmita reached the quarterfinals after defeating USA’s Ishika Jaiswal 22-20, 21-15 in the Round of 16. Meanwhile, India’s Anmol Kharb bowed out of the tournament after losing to China’s fourth seed Han Qianxi 14-21, 21-15, 21-13 in the quarterfinals.

title: Akshara Gupta scores triple century in Bihar Cricket Association tournament
summary: In the Women’s Under-19 One-Day Trophy organized by the Bihar Cricket Association in Bhagalpur, 19-year-old batsman Akshara Gupta delivered a spectacular performance, scoring an unbeaten 306 runs off just 126 balls. In her innings, Akshara smashed 55...
body: In the Women’s Under-19 One-Day Trophy organized by the Bihar Cricket Association in Bhagalpur, 19-year-old batsman Akshara Gupta delivered a spectacular performance, scoring an unbeaten 306 runs off just 126 balls.
