In her innings, Akshara smashed 55 boundaries and 8 sixes. In domestic cricket, this is one of the biggest innings in the history of women’s cricket, second only to Mumbai’s Ira Gupta. Propelled by Akshara’s explosive batting, her team posted a mammoth total of 450 runs in 40 overs. In response, the opposing team was bundled out for a mere 121 runs.

title: India defeat Uruguay 3-2, top Pool A in FIH Women’s Nations Cup
summary: In Hockey, India defeated Uruguay 3-2 to register their third consecutive win and finish on top of Pool A in the FIH Women’s Nations Cup in New Zealand today. After conceding an early goal, India staged a strong comeback with Deepika scoring twice and Deepika...
body: In Hockey, India defeated Uruguay 3-2 to register their third consecutive win and finish on top of Pool A in the FIH Women’s Nations Cup in New Zealand today. After conceding an early goal, India staged a strong comeback with Deepika scoring twice and Deepika Soreng adding another goal. Uruguay had taken the lead through Chiara Appennino in the 13th minute, before Deepika equalised through a penalty corner in the 24th minute. Soreng then put India ahead with a field goal in the 43rd minute. Uruguay captain Manuela Vilar restored parity in the 55th minute, but Deepika struck again a minute later to seal the victory for India. With three wins from three matches, India topped Pool A and advanced to the semifinals, where they will face Chile on Saturday. The match also marked a significant milestone for Neha, who completed 200 international appearances for India.

title: MoS Raksha Khadse reviews preparations for Asian Senior Fencing Championships in Delhi
summary: Minister of State for Youth Affairs and Sports, Raksha Khadse today reviewed preparations for the Asian Senior Fencing Championships 2026 which will get underway in New Delhi tomorrow. Youth Affairs and Sports Minister Dr. Mansukh Mandaviya will be present at...
body: Minister of State for Youth Affairs and Sports, Raksha Khadse today reviewed preparations for the Asian Senior Fencing Championships 2026 which will get underway in New Delhi tomorrow. Youth Affairs and Sports Minister Dr. Mansukh Mandaviya will be present at the opening Ceremony which is themed One Dream, One Blade, One Pride. The theme celebrates the spirit of fencing, sporting excellence and international unity. During her visit, Ms Khadse reviewed the competition infrastructure, athlete facilities, and technology-enabled officiating systems and also took stock of the advanced sports technologies and world-class infrastructure being deployed for the event. On the occasion, Ms Khadse said that under the leadership of Prime Minister Narendra Modi, India is rapidly emerging as a global sporting nation. She emphasised that the Asian Senior Fencing Championships 2026 reflect this vision and showcase India’s growing capabilities on the global sporting stage. The Minister further noted that international competitions of this scale provide valuable exposure to athletes, encourage sporting excellence and contribute significantly to building a vibrant and future-ready sporting ecosystem. Ms Khadse highlighted that such events strengthen India’s position as a preferred destination for global sporting competitions while creating new opportunities for Indian athletes to compete alongside the best in the world. She appreciated the efforts of the Fencing Association of India, technical officials and all stakeholders involved in organising the event. This year’s championships mark a historic milestone as India is hosting the prestigious continental championship for the first time. The event will witness participation from 36 countries, with a total of 787 accreditations, including athletes, coaches and others.

title: Hockey: India beat World Champions Germany 3-1 in FIH Pro League
summary: In hockey, Indian men’s team stunned reigning world champions Germany with a 3-1 victory in the FIH Pro League in Rotterdam, the Netherlands, last night. Thirty-three-year-old Manpreet Singh became India’s most-capped hockey player during the match, making his...
body: In hockey, Indian men’s team stunned reigning world champions Germany with a 3-1 victory in the FIH Pro League in Rotterdam, the Netherlands, last night. Thirty-three-year-old Manpreet Singh became India’s most-capped hockey player during the match, making his 413th appearance for the country. He surpassed former captain and current Hockey India President Dilip Tirkey, who represented India 412 times in his illustrious career. India took a 2-0 lead at half-time, with goals from Mandeep Singh in the 7th minute and Shilanand Lakra in the 13th minute. Nilakanta Sharma extended India’s lead with a goal in the 35th minute. Germany managed to pull one back through Raphael Hartkopf in the 45th minute, but India held on to secure a 3-1 win. India will face Germany again today in the second leg of the fixture before taking on the Netherlands on Sunday. In the following week, India will play Pakistan and England twice to conclude their Pro League campaign. Earlier, India had suffered a 2-3 defeat against Olympic champions the Netherlands on the 14th of this month.

title: Badminton: Ashmita Chaliha, Anmol Kharb advance to women’s singles quarter-finals of Macau Open
summary: In Badminton, India’s Ashmita Chaliha and Anmol Kharb advanced to the women’s singles quarter-finals of the Macau Open in Macau, China, today. Ashmita booked her place in the last eight after beating USA’s world No. 65 Ishika Jaiswal 22-20, 21-15 in the Round...
body: In Badminton, India’s Ashmita Chaliha and Anmol Kharb advanced to the women’s singles quarter-finals of the Macau Open in Macau, China, today. Ashmita booked her place in the last eight after beating USA’s world No. 65 Ishika Jaiswal 22-20, 21-15 in the Round of 16. Meanwhile, 19-year-old Anmol Kharb also progressed to the quarter-finals after Malaysia’s Goh Jin Wei retired from their pre quarterfinal clash. Anmol was leading 12-4 in the opening game when the match was called off.

title: Minakshi Hooda advances to quarter-finals of World Boxing Cup 2026 in China
summary: India’s Minakshi Hooda advanced to the quarter-finals of the World Boxing Cup 2026 in Guiyang, China, today. Minakshi secured a convincing 5-0 victory over Poland’s Natalia Kuczewska in the women’s 51kg round of 16 bout. Four Indian boxers will be in quarter...
body: India’s Minakshi Hooda advanced to the quarter-finals of the World Boxing Cup 2026 in Guiyang, China, today. Minakshi secured a convincing 5-0 victory over Poland’s Natalia Kuczewska in the women’s 51kg round of 16 bout. Four Indian boxers will be in quarter final action tomorrow. In the women’s 51kg category, Minakshi will face Kazakhstan’s Alua Balkibekova, while Saneh will take on Poland’s Kinga Krowka in the women’s 65kg division. Prachi is set to meet Chinese Taipei’s Chengyu Yang in the women’s 57kg quarter-finals. In the men’s competition, Nikhil will face Azerbaijan’s Amin Mammadzada in the 55kg category. Meanwhile, In womens 48 kg category Jyoti and Jugnoo in 85kg division have already assured India of podium finishes after advancing to the semi-finals.

title: FIH Women’s Nations Cup: India beat Uruguay 3-2 to top Pool A
summary: In Hockey, India defeated Uruguay 3-2 to register their third consecutive win and finish on top of Pool A in the FIH Women’s Nations Cup in New Zealand today. After conceding an early goal, India staged a strong comeback with Deepika scoring twice and Deepika ...
body: In Hockey, India defeated Uruguay 3-2 to register their third consecutive win and finish on top of Pool A in the FIH Women’s Nations Cup in New Zealand today. After conceding an early goal, India staged a strong comeback with Deepika scoring twice and Deepika Soreng adding another goal. Uruguay had taken the lead through Chiara Appennino in the 13th minute before Deepika equalised through a penalty corner in the 24th minute. Soreng then put India ahead with a field goal in the 43rd minute. Uruguay captain Manuela Vilar restored parity in the 55th minute, but Deepika struck again a minute later to seal the victory for India. With three wins from three matches, India topped Pool A and advanced to the semifinals, where they will face the winner of the match between Chile and Korea on June 20. The match also marked a significant milestone for Neha, who completed 200 international appearances for India.

title: 2nd ODI: India beat Afghanistan by 170 runs in three-match series
summary: In Men’s Cricket, India defeated Afghanistan by 170 runs in the second ODI of the three-match series in Lucknow to take an unassailable 2-nil lead. Put in to bat first, India posted a massive 402 runs in 49.5 overs before being bowled out, powered by Shubman G...
body: In Men’s Cricket, India defeated Afghanistan by 170 runs in the second ODI of the three-match series in Lucknow to take an unassailable 2-nil lead. Put in to bat first, India posted a massive 402 runs in 49.5 overs before being bowled out, powered by Shubman Gill’s 154 and Ishan Kishan’s 125. For Afghanistan, Nangeyalia Kharoti picked up four wickets, while Rashid Khan claimed three. In reply, Afghanistan were bundled out for 232 in 44.3 overs. For India, Arshdeep Singh and Gurnoor Brar each picked up three wickets, while Prince Yadav claimed two. Shubman Gill was adjudged the player of the match. The third and last match will be played in Chennai on Saturday.

title: India defeat Japan 2-1 to reach semi-finals of FIH Hockey Women’s Nations Cup New Zealand 2026
summary: In Women’s Hockey, India defeated Japan 2-1 in their second Pool A match of the FIH Hockey Women’s Nations Cup New Zealand 2026 at the North Harbour National Hockey Centre in Auckland yesterday. With this win, India secured a spot in the semi-finals, having wo...
body: In Women’s Hockey, India defeated Japan 2-1 in their second Pool A match of the FIH Hockey Women’s Nations Cup New Zealand 2026 at the North Harbour National Hockey Centre in Auckland yesterday. With this win, India secured a spot in the semi-finals, having won both their pool games so far. Captain and Player of the Match Salima Tete and Lalremsiami netted the goals for India, while Hiramitsu Ai was the lone goalscorer for Japan. It was a memorable game for Indian midfielder Jyoti, who marked her 100th senior international appearance. The win took unbeaten India to the top of Pool A with six points from wins against the USA and Japan. India will next face Uruguay in their last Pool A match of the tournament tomorrow at 4:15 AM Indian Time. Japan and the USA will also face off for the second semi-final spot from Pool A. All FIH Hockey Women’s Nations Cup matches will be streamed live on Watch.Hockey.

title: AIFF launches inaugural Under-17 Women’s Youth League in India
summary: The All India Football Federation (AIFF) yesterday announced the inaugural edition of the AIFF Under-17 Women’s Youth League. The tournament will kick off on the 18th of next month at the RKM Football Academy in Narainpur, Chhattisgarh, marking a historic mile...
body: The All India Football Federation (AIFF) yesterday announced the inaugural edition of the AIFF Under-17 Women’s Youth League. The tournament will kick off on the 18th of next month at the RKM Football Academy in Narainpur, Chhattisgarh, marking a historic milestone for women’s youth club football in India. The final match will be played on the 29th of July. The fixtures for the league will be announced tomorrow. The tournament marks the first national youth league for Under-17 women’s clubs in the country, featuring 16 teams from across India. The participating teams have been divided into four groups of four, with the top two sides from each group advancing to the quarter-finals before the competition progresses through the knockout stage. Among the 16 participating clubs, there are five teams from the Indian Women’s League (IWL) and seven from the IWL 2. The four remaining teams are KMP Aspire FC, Zinc Football Academy, PFC Kerala, and hosts RKM Football Academy. The inaugural league marks the first opportunity for clubs across the country to field their Under-17 women’s teams in a structured national tournament. The launch of this league represents another significant step in the AIFF’s commitment to strengthening the women’s football development pathway. Meanwhile, the RKM Football Academy in Narainpur has emerged as one of India’s leading football venues in recent years, regularly hosting AIFF national competitions across men’s and women’s football in both youth and senior categories. The venue also successfully hosted the Rajmata Jijabai Trophy last season and will now welcome another landmark competition to its growing portfolio.

title: India set target of 403 runs for Afghanistan in second ODI in Lucknow
summary: In Cricket, India have set a target of 403 runs for Afghanistan in the second ODI of the three-match series in Lucknow. India were bowled out for 402 runs in 49.5 overs. Both Shubman Gill and Ishan Kishan scored centuries. Gill made 154 runs, while Kishan cont...
body: In Cricket, India have set a target of 403 runs for Afghanistan in the second ODI of the three-match series in Lucknow. India were bowled out for 402 runs in 49.5 overs. Both Shubman Gill and Ishan Kishan scored centuries. Gill made 154 runs, while Kishan contributed 125. For Afghanistan, Nangeyalia Kharoti picked up 4 wickets, while Rashid Khan claimed 3 wickets. Earlier, Afghanistan won the toss and elected to field. The Shubman Gill-led Men in Blue are currently leading the series 1-nil and will look to clinch it in today’s match. In the series opener, played in Dharamsala on June 13, India defeated Afghanistan by seven wickets in a rain-affected contest.

title: Norway open FIFA World Cup 2026 campaign with dominant 4–1 win over Iraq in Group I
summary: In Football, Norway returned to the FIFA World Cup in style with a 4-1 win over Iraq in their Group I opener at the Boston Stadium in the United States this morning. Norway’s Erling Haaland netted twice to make his World Cup debut special. The scoreline now pu...
body: In Football, Norway returned to the FIFA World Cup in style with a 4-1 win over Iraq in their Group I opener at the Boston Stadium in the United States this morning. Norway’s Erling Haaland netted twice to make his World Cup debut special. The scoreline now puts Norway at the top of Group I. Earlier, France defeated Senegal 3-1 in the opening game of Group I of the FIFA World Cup 2026 at New Jersey Stadium last night. Kylian Mbappe scored a brilliant brace to become France’s all-time leading scorer with 58 goals. He also surpassed Lionel Messi in the all-time World Cup scoring charts. In the game, Senegal matched France in a tightly contested first half, but the breakthrough came just after the hour mark when Michael Olise played a precise pass into Mbappe, who calmly finished to open the scoring. Paris Saint-Germain forward Bradley Barcola doubled the lead in the 79th minute, delicately lifting the ball over Edouard Mendy after coming off the bench, before Ibrahim Mbaye pulled one back for Senegal to reduce the deficit. The next target for Mbappe will be Miroslav Klose’s record 16 World Cup finals goals. The Frenchman currently has 14 in the global showpiece. Earlier, Iran secured a hard-fought 2-2 draw against New Zealand in their opening match at SoFi Stadium in Los Angeles. In another fixture, Uruguay and Saudi Arabia played out a 1-1 draw in their opening clash in Miami. Belgium and Egypt also shared the points with a 1-1 draw in their Group G opener at the Seattle Stadium.

title: FAI announces 24-member squad for the 26th Senior Asian Fencing Championships 2026
summary: The Fencing Association of India (FAI) has officially announced the Indian senior team for the 26th Senior Asian Fencing Championships 2026, which will be held in Delhi from June 19 to 24. Marking a historic milestone, India will host the prestigious continent...
body: The Fencing Association of India (FAI) has officially announced the Indian senior team for the 26th Senior Asian Fencing Championships 2026, which will be held in Delhi from June 19 to 24. Marking a historic milestone, India will host the prestigious continental event for the first time. Over 30 countries and more than 400 top athletes will participate in this high-stakes competition. India’s 24-member squad comprises 12 women and 12 men and will be led by C.A. Bhavani Devi, India’s first and only fencing Olympian.

title: Sports Minister Mansukh Mandaviya to lead Fit India Cyclothon in Mumbai on June 17
summary: Union Minister for Youth Affairs and Sports Dr Mansukh Mandaviya will lead a special Fit India Cyclothon at Mumbai’s Worli Cycle Track tomorrow. The Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports said that the FIT India Cyclothon is part of the broader Fit India Movemen...
body: Union Minister for Youth Affairs and Sports Dr Mansukh Mandaviya will lead a special Fit India Cyclothon at Mumbai’s Worli Cycle Track tomorrow. The Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports said that the FIT India Cyclothon is part of the broader Fit India Movement, which continues to promote fitness as a way of life through community participation, cycling, yoga and other physical activities. Around 250 cyclists from different walks of life will pedal alongside the Minister against the backdrop of Mumbai’s scenic coastline. It will also serve as a curtain-raiser to the global celebrations of the 12th International Day of Yoga on the 21st of June.

title: Hockey Nations Cup: Women team storms into semifinal, defeating Japan 2-1
summary: In Women’s Hockey, India has stormed into the semi-finals of the FIH Hockey Nations Cup in Auckland, New Zealand. India defeated Japan 2-1 in their second Pool A match. Captain and Player of the Match Salima Tete and Lalremsiami netted the goals for India, whi...
body: In Women’s Hockey, India has stormed into the semi-finals of the FIH Hockey Nations Cup in Auckland, New Zealand. India defeated Japan 2-1 in their second Pool A match. Captain and Player of the Match Salima Tete and Lalremsiami netted the goals for India, while Hiramitsu Ai was the lone goalscorer for Japan. It was a memorable game for midfielder Jyoti, who marked her 100th senior international appearance. The win took unbeaten India to the top of Pool A with six points from wins against the USA and Japan. India will next face Uruguay in their last Pool A match of the tournament on Thursday, while Japan and the USA will face off for the second semi-final spot from Pool A. Meanwhile, in Men’s Hockey, India will face Germany in FIH Hockey Pro League in Rotterdam, Netherlands. Earlier, in the first match of the tour India suffered a narrow 2-3 loss against the Netherlands in Rotterdam on Sunday.

title: Sri Lanka cricket Tri series: Pacer Asho Sharma replaces injured Yudhvir Singh in India A squad
summary: Pacer Ashok Sharma has been included in the India A squad for the ongoing tri-series in Sri Lanka as a replacement for injured Yudhvir Singh. Yudhvir reported discomfort in his right shoulder while bowling and also experienced pain earlier during a fielding se...
body: Pacer Ashok Sharma has been included in the India A squad for the ongoing tri-series in Sri Lanka as a replacement for injured Yudhvir Singh. Yudhvir reported discomfort in his right shoulder while bowling and also experienced pain earlier during a fielding session. The BCCI said, the medical team has recommended that Yudhvir undergo a graduated rehabilitation programme under the supervision of the BCCI Centre of Excellence to achieve a full recovery from the right rotator cuff injury. India A have lost two games in a row in the tri-series, falling to Afghanistan A and host nation Sri Lanka A. India A will next take on Afghanistan A at Rangiri Dambulla International Stadium in Dambulla, Sri Lanka tomorrow.

title: ICC Women’s T20: India hand Pakistan a crushing defeat
summary: In ICC Women’s T20 World Cup, India defeated Pakistan by 64 runs in a Group A cricket match at Edgbaston in Birmingham. India set a target of 171 runs for arch rivals Pakistan. In reply Pakistan were all out with 106 runs in 17 overs. Opting to bat, India post...
body: In ICC Women’s T20 World Cup, India defeated Pakistan by 64 runs in a Group A cricket match at Edgbaston in Birmingham. India set a target of 171 runs for arch rivals Pakistan. In reply Pakistan were all out with 106 runs in 17 overs. Opting to bat, India posted 170 for six in their allotted 20 overs. Smriti Mandhana was the highest run scorer with 68 runs in 44 deliveries. Earlier Indian skipper Harmanpreet Kaur won the toss and elected to bat. Deepti Sharma was adjudged as the player of the match. In the other fixture of the day, Bangladesh registered a convincing six-wicket victory over the Netherlands. Opting to bat, Netherland posted 138 for eight in their allotted 20 overs. In response, Bangladesh chased down the target comfortably, scoring 141 for 4 in 19.1 overs. Juairiya Ferdous was awarded player of the match for her match winning knock of 50 runs in 33 balls. The month-long tournament is being played across England and Wales, with the final set to be held at Lord’s Cricket Ground on 5th July.

title: India to begin ICC Women’s T20 World Cup campaign against Pakistan at Edgbaston today
summary: In the ICC Women’s T20 World Cup, India will begin their campaign against arch-rivals Pakistan in a Group A fixture at Edgbaston in Birmingham today. The match will start at 7 PM Indian Standard Time. India holds a strong record against Pakistan in Women’s T20...
body: In the ICC Women’s T20 World Cup, India will begin their campaign against arch-rivals Pakistan in a Group A fixture at Edgbaston in Birmingham today. The match will start at 7 PM Indian Standard Time. India holds a strong record against Pakistan in Women’s T20 Internationals, winning 13 of their 16 encounters. In Another Clash today, debutants Netherlands will take on Bangladesh. The match will start at 10:30 AM Indian Standard Time. Earlier, West Indies beat defending champions New Zealand by 7 wickets in a Group B clash in Southampton, England today. New Zealand posted 162 for the loss of 6 wickets. Chasing the target, West Indies reached their target with a ball to spare as Shemaine Campbelle finished unbeaten on 90. In another match, Australia began their T20 World Cup campaign, registering a 65-run victory over South Africa in group A clash at Old Trafford, in Manchester yesterday. Australia battled to put up 172 for the loss of 8 wickets. Chasing the target, the Proteas posted 107 in 16.4 overs. Georgia Wareham was judged Player of the Match for her all-round display, striking 32 off 22 balls. In Group B action, Scotland defeated Ireland by 40 runs at Old Trafford in Manchester yesterday. Batting first, Scotland scored 161 for 5 in 20 overs. Ireland were then bowled out for 121 in 19.1 overs. Scotland captain Kathryn Bryce was named Player of the Match for her all-round performance, scoring 60 runs and taking two wickets. Earlier in the tournament opener, hosts England beat Sri Lanka by 87 runs at Edgbaston on Friday. The month-long competition is being held across England and Wales, with the final scheduled at Lord’s Cricket Ground on the 5th of next month.

title: India defeat Afghanistan by seven wickets in rain-affected first ODI in Dharamshala
summary: In Men’s Cricket, India defeated Afghanistan by seven wickets in a rain-affected first One Day International of the three-match series in Dharamshala, Himachal Pradesh, last night. Chasing 195 runs, India overhauled the target in 22.5 overs. Indian Skipper Shu...
body: In Men’s Cricket, India defeated Afghanistan by seven wickets in a rain-affected first One Day International of the three-match series in Dharamshala, Himachal Pradesh, last night. Chasing 195 runs, India overhauled the target in 22.5 overs. Indian Skipper Shubman Gill scored unbeaten 84 off 66 balls. Earlier, put into bat, Afghanistan were bowled out for 194 in 24.5 overs. Shubman Gill was adjudged player of the match. For Afghanistan, Rahmanullah Gurbaz smashed a brilliant century, scoring 102 off 51 balls. For India, debutant Harsh Dubey and Gurnoor Brar clinched three wickets each. The match was reduced to 25 overs-per-side after relentless rains delayed the start. India won the toss and elected to field first. The 2nd ODI between India and Afghanistan is scheduled for 17th June at the Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium in Lucknow.

title: Australia lead Turkiye 1-0 at half-time in opening FIFA World Cup 2026 match
summary: Australia lead Turkiye 1-0 at half-time in their opening FIFA World Cup 2026 match in Vancouver. Nestory Irankunda scored the only goal of the first half, finishing a quick counter-attack after Turkiye had dominated possession in the early stages. In other FIF...
body: Australia lead Turkiye 1-0 at half-time in their opening FIFA World Cup 2026 match in Vancouver. Nestory Irankunda scored the only goal of the first half, finishing a quick counter-attack after Turkiye had dominated possession in the early stages. In other FIFA World Cup action today, Scotland ended a 28-year wait for a World Cup victory with a hard-fought 1-0 win over Haiti in their Group C opener at Gillette Stadium in Massachusetts. Elsewhere in the same group, five-time champions Brazil were held to a 1-1 draw by Morocco in New Jersey. Vinícius Júnior rescued a point for Brazil with an equaliser in the 32nd minute. Meanwhile, the Group B encounter between Qatar and Switzerland also finished level at 1-1 in California, with both sides settling for a share of the points in their opening match of the tournament.

title: Turkiye: India’s Dhiraj Bommadevara and Kumkum Mohod Clinch Gold at Archery World Cup Stage 3
summary: In Archery, India’s Dhiraj Bommadevara and teenager Kumkum Mohod clinched the gold medal in recurve mixed team event at the World Cup Stage 3 at Antalya in Turkiye today. The Indian duo produced a brilliant display to upset reigning Olympic champions Oh Ye-jin...
body: In Archery, India’s Dhiraj Bommadevara and teenager Kumkum Mohod clinched the gold medal in recurve mixed team event at the World Cup Stage 3 at Antalya in Turkiye today. The Indian duo produced a brilliant display to upset reigning Olympic champions Oh Ye-jin and Kim Je-deok of South Korea 5-1 in the finals. It was the first World Cup gold medal for both Dhiraj and Kumkum in the mixed team event. Meanwhile, In Men’s Individual event, Dhiraj stuns Paris Olympic medalist Lee Woo Seok of South Korea 6-4 in the final to bag the gold medal. He is now just the third Indian archer to ever win the men’s recurve individual world cup title. It was the first time India had won two gold medals at a single World Cup stage. The performance helped India finish second in medals tally behind China while Korea were pushed to fourth spot. Mexico were third.

title: Australian Open: Akane Yamaguchi of Japan wins women’s singles semi-finals
summary: In Badminton, India’s campaign at the Australian Badminton Open 2026 came to an end today after two-time Olympic medallist PV Sindhu bowed out in the women’s singles semi-finals. Sindhu suffered a straight-games defeat against top seed and world number three A...
body: In Badminton, India’s campaign at the Australian Badminton Open 2026 came to an end today after two-time Olympic medallist PV Sindhu bowed out in the women’s singles semi-finals. Sindhu suffered a straight-games defeat against top seed and world number three Akane Yamaguchi of Japan at the Quaycentre in Sydney. The tournament’s third seed fought hard in a closely contested opening game before Yamaguchi prevailed 22-20. The Japanese shuttler then dominated the second game to seal a 21-12 victory in the 43-minute encounter and advance to the final.

title: India to begin Women’s T20 World Cup campaign against Pakistan tomorrow
summary: In the ICC Women’s T20 World Cup, the Indian women’s team will begin its campaign against arch-rivals Pakistan at Edgbaston in Birmingham tomorrow. India has a dominant record against Pakistan in T20 Internationals, leading the head-to-head contest with 13 win...
body: In the ICC Women’s T20 World Cup, the Indian women’s team will begin its campaign against arch-rivals Pakistan at Edgbaston in Birmingham tomorrow. India has a dominant record against Pakistan in T20 Internationals, leading the head-to-head contest with 13 wins from 16 matches. Yesterday, in the opening match of the tournament, hosts England defeated Sri Lanka by 87 runs at Edgbaston in Birmingham. The month-long tournament is being hosted across England and Wales, while the final will be played on July 5 at the iconic Lord’s Cricket Ground.

title: India defeat Afghanistan by seven wickets in rain-hit first ODI in Dharamshala
summary: In Men’s Cricket, India defeated Afghanistan by seven wickets in a rain affected first One Day International of the three-match series in Dharamshala, Himachal Pradesh. Chasing 195 runs, India overhauled the target in 22.5 overs. Indian Skipper Shubhman Gill s...
body: In Men’s Cricket, India defeated Afghanistan by seven wickets in a rain affected first One Day International of the three-match series in Dharamshala, Himachal Pradesh. Chasing 195 runs, India overhauled the target in 22.5 overs. Indian Skipper Shubhman Gill scored unbeaten 84 off 66 balls. Earlier put into bat, Afghanistan were bowled out for 194 in 24.5 overs. Rahmanullah Gurbaz smashed a brilliant century, scoring 102 off 51 balls. For India, debutant Harsh Dubey and Gurnoor Brar clinched three wickets each. The match was reduced to 25 overs-per-side after relentless rains delayed the start. India won the toss and elected to field first.

title: Stage set for biggest edition of ICC Women’s T20 World Cup; 12 teams in race
summary: The 10th edition of the ICC Women’s T20 Cricket World Cup will begin today, with hosts England taking on Sri Lanka in the opening match of the tournament at Edgbaston, Birmingham. The match is scheduled to begin at 11:00 PM Indian standard time. The month-long...
body: The 10th edition of the ICC Women’s T20 Cricket World Cup will begin today, with hosts England taking on Sri Lanka in the opening match of the tournament at Edgbaston, Birmingham. The match is scheduled to begin at 11:00 PM Indian standard time. The month-long tournament will be hosted across England and Wales while the final is set to take place on July 5 at the iconic Lord’s Cricket Ground. Indian Women’s team will start their campaign against arch rivals Pakistan on Sunday. Harmanpreet Kaur led Indian team will be looking to create history as they aim for their first T20 World Cup title. For the first time in the tournament’s history, 12 teams will compete, with 33 matches scheduled across seven venues, making it the biggest edition of the ICC Women’s T20 World Cup to date.

title: Sindhu storms into semi-finals of Australia Open
summary: In Badminton, PV Sindhu has stormed into the women’s singles semi-finals of the Australian Open tournament in Sydney. The third-seeded Indian outclassed Chinese Taipei’s Chen Su Yu 21-6, 21-9 in a one-sided quarter-final clash today. Sindhu will next face top ...
body: In Badminton, PV Sindhu has stormed into the women’s singles semi-finals of the Australian Open tournament in Sydney. The third-seeded Indian outclassed Chinese Taipei’s Chen Su Yu 21-6, 21-9 in a one-sided quarter-final clash today. Sindhu will next face top seed Akane Yamaguchi of Japan in the semi-final match. Yamaguchi defeated India’s Tanvi Sharma 21-14, 21-14 in the Quarter-final clash today. The Indian men’s doubles pair of Hariharan Amsakarunan and M. R. Arjun bowed out in the quarterfinals after retiring from their match while trailing 19-21, 9-16 against Chinese Taipei’s Chen Cheng Kuan and Liu Kuang Heng.

title: Former New Zealand captain announces his retirement from international cricket
summary: Former New Zealand captain and batting great Kane Williamson today announced his retirement from international cricket after a glorious career spanning more than 15 years. The 35-year finishes as New Zealand’s all-time leading international run-scorer across a...
body: Former New Zealand captain and batting great Kane Williamson today announced his retirement from international cricket after a glorious career spanning more than 15 years. The 35-year finishes as New Zealand’s all-time leading international run-scorer across all formats with 19,346 runs, including over 9500 runs in Test cricket with 33 centuries and six double tons. Captaining the side in all three formats, Williamson led New Zealand to the finals of the 2019 World Cup and 2021 T20 World Cup, and guided them as they won the inaugural World Test Championship in 2021. In a statement issued by New Zealand Cricket, Williamson said, the time has come to end his international journey completely. Reflecting on his association with the national side, he added that representing New Zealand had been the greatest honour of his career. This move marks the end of an era in New Zealand cricket across all three formats of the game, following his earlier decision to step way from T20 Internationals last year.

title: Former Asian Games gold medallist Jaspal Rana passes away; President, PM Modi express grief
summary: Renowned Indian shooting coach and former Asian Games gold medallist Jaspal Rana has passed away. He was 49. According to National Rifle Association of India (NRAI) president Kalikesh Narayan Singh Deo, Rana breathed his last at a Delhi hospital last night. Ra...
body: Renowned Indian shooting coach and former Asian Games gold medallist Jaspal Rana has passed away. He was 49. According to National Rifle Association of India (NRAI) president Kalikesh Narayan Singh Deo, Rana breathed his last at a Delhi hospital last night. Rana recently underwent a medical procedure after falling ill during the Indian contingent’s return flight from the ISSF World Cup in Munich, Germany. He mentored Manu Bhaker and helped her secure a historic double bronze medal at the 2024 Paris Olympics. President Droupadi Murmu, Prime Minister Narendra Modi, and other leaders have expressed grief over the passing of Jaspal Rana.

title: PV Sindhu storms into Australian Open semi-finals with dominant win
summary: In badminton, India’s PV Sindhu stormed into the women’s singles semi-finals of the Australian Open tournament in Sydney today. The third-seeded Indian outclassed Chinese Taipei’s Chen Su Yu 21–6, 21–9 in a one-sided quarter-final clash. Sindhu will next face ...
body: In badminton, India’s PV Sindhu stormed into the women’s singles semi-finals of the Australian Open tournament in Sydney today. The third-seeded Indian outclassed Chinese Taipei’s Chen Su Yu 21–6, 21–9 in a one-sided quarter-final clash. Sindhu will next face top seed Akane Yamaguchi of Japan in the semi-finals. Earlier, Yamaguchi defeated India’s Tanvi Sharma 21–14, 21–14 in the quarter-final today.

title: PM Modi praises performances of Indian athletes, calls for ambitious Olympic targets
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has praised the performances of Indian sportspersons and urged the nation to set ambitious targets for the Olympics. Speaking at the NDA Conclave in New Delhi, Mr Modi highlighted the achievements of young athletes and the rapid...
body: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has praised the performances of Indian sportspersons and urged the nation to set ambitious targets for the Olympics. Speaking at the NDA Conclave in New Delhi, Mr Modi highlighted the achievements of young athletes and the rapid growth of sports in India while stressing the need to aim higher on the global stage. Prime Minister said that Indian youth are performing remarkably well in sports, and their performances continue to improve with each passing day. Mr Modi noted that India is increasingly emerging as a major hub for professional sports and expressed confidence that the country’s standing in the sporting world will continue to rise. He said India is steadily becoming the home of some of the world’s biggest sporting leagues, and this reputation will grow even stronger in the years ahead. Prime Minister said that our preparations for the Olympics are also a reflection of India’s aspirations. Emphasising the need for a bold approach, Mr Modi urged athletes and stakeholders to dream big and work relentlessly towards achieving success. Prime Minister also used the occasion to underline his government’s commitment to reforms, drawing a parallel between India’s progress in sports and its broader development agenda.

title: 2026 FIFA World Cup kicks off at Estadio Azteca in Mexico City
summary: Sports fans across the world are set for a month-long football spectacle as the 2026 FIFA World Cup kicks off tonight at the historic Estadio Azteca in Mexico City. In the opening match, hosts Mexico will take on South Africa in a Group A clash. The match begi...
body: Sports fans across the world are set for a month-long football spectacle as the 2026 FIFA World Cup kicks off tonight at the historic Estadio Azteca in Mexico City. In the opening match, hosts Mexico will take on South Africa in a Group A clash. The match begins at 12:30 AM Indian Standard Time. Interestingly, the fixture brings back memories of the 2010 World Cup opener, which also featured the same two sides. This edition of the World Cup will be the biggest in history, with an expansion from 32 to 48 teams. A record 104 matches will be played, with 72 in the group stage and 32 in the knockout rounds, across host nations the United States, Canada, and Mexico. The three host nations qualified automatically, while 45 teams earned their places through a demanding two-year qualification journey. The competition features 12 groups of four teams each. The top two teams from every group, along with the eight best third-placed teams, will advance to the Round of 32. This expands the knockout stage to 32 teams for the first time in tournament history. The grand final is scheduled for 19th of July, promising a thrilling conclusion to football’s greatest celebration. This World Cup is also expected to be one of the most challenging in history, with teams facing demanding conditions across three countries – including long travel distances, varying climates, different altitudes, and shifting time zones. Over the decades, only eight nations have lifted the FIFA World Cup trophy – Brazil, Italy, Germany, Spain, Argentina, England, France, and Uruguay, the inaugural champions. Among the most unfortunate sides in World Cup history remain the Netherlands, who have reached three finals but are yet to win the title despite producing generations of world-class talent. From history to high stakes, from legends to new challengers – the stage is set for football’s ultimate battle.

title: India A beat Sri Lanka A by 8 runs in Tri-Nation A Series opener
summary: In Men’s Cricket, India A defeated Sri Lanka A by 8 runs in a close contest in the opening clash of the ‘Tri-Nation A Series’ at the Rangiri Dambulla International Stadium in Sri Lanka yesterday. Opting to bat first, India scored 277 for 6 in the stipulated 50...
body: In Men’s Cricket, India A defeated Sri Lanka A by 8 runs in a close contest in the opening clash of the ‘Tri-Nation A Series’ at the Rangiri Dambulla International Stadium in Sri Lanka yesterday. Opting to bat first, India scored 277 for 6 in the stipulated 50 overs, riding on Ruturaj Gaikwad’s 101 runs. Skipper Tilak Varma also scored 60 runs. For Sri Lanka, Mohamed Shiraz bagged 2 wickets. In the second innings, Sri Lanka fell short of just 8 runs as they got bundled out for 269 runs, with 7 balls remaining. For India, Arshad Khan, Anukul Roy, Ayush Badoni and Vipraj Nigam each picked up 2 wickets. Ruturaj Gaikwad was adjudged Player of the Match for his tonne. India A will next face Afghanistan A tomorrow at the same venue. All the matches will be played in Dambulla, with the final match scheduled on the 21st of this month.

title: Afghanistan ODI series: Indian squad suffers another setback; Hardik ruled out of series
summary: India has suffered yet another blow to their ODI squad ahead of the three-match ODI series against Afghanistan. After Virat Kohli, Hardik Pandya has been ruled out of the series, which is set to begin India’s preparations for the 2027 ODI World Cup, to be held...
body: India has suffered yet another blow to their ODI squad ahead of the three-match ODI series against Afghanistan. After Virat Kohli, Hardik Pandya has been ruled out of the series, which is set to begin India’s preparations for the 2027 ODI World Cup, to be held in South Africa. According to reports, Hardik is suffering from a quadriceps injury, which will take three weeks to heal.

title: Mohammed Siraj withdrawn from India’s squads for Ireland and England T20I series
summary: Fast bowler Mohammed Siraj has been withdrawn from India’s squads for the upcoming T20 International series against Ireland and England. The Men’s Selection Committee has named Prasidh Krishna as Siraj’s replacement for both T20I series. In a statement, the BC...
body: Fast bowler Mohammed Siraj has been withdrawn from India’s squads for the upcoming T20 International series against Ireland and England. The Men’s Selection Committee has named Prasidh Krishna as Siraj’s replacement for both T20I series. In a statement, the BCCI said that its Medical Team and the Team Management advised Siraj a period of rest as part of his workload management programme. The decision has been taken as a precautionary measure to ensure adequate recovery ahead of a long international season.

title: 22 rowers inducted in TOPS to build sustainable bench strength for 2026 Asian Games
summary: 22 rowers have been inducted into the Target Olympic Podium Scheme (TOPS) Development Group ahead of the 2026 Asian Games. A total of 22 rowers are now a part of the TOPS Development group across six boats. Ministry for Youth Affairs and Sports said that the i...
body: 22 rowers have been inducted into the Target Olympic Podium Scheme (TOPS) Development Group ahead of the 2026 Asian Games. A total of 22 rowers are now a part of the TOPS Development group across six boats. Ministry for Youth Affairs and Sports said that the induction of these rowers is another effort to build sustainable bench strength across several boat categories rather than focusing only on individual medal prospects. The crew will now prepare for major international competitions featuring Rowing, including the Asian Games and World Cup circuit. Among those inducted are Paris 2024 Olympian Balraj Panwar, Asian Games 2022 silver medalist Arvind Singh, and Asian Games 2022 double medalist Jaswinder Singh. While the majority of the inducted athletes are being prepared with a direct focus on the Asian Games 2026 cycle, some crews are also part of India’s broader international development pathway targeting future competitions, like the Commonwealth Games 2030 set to be hosted in the country.

title: India’s women wrestlers win 10 medals at Under-17 Asian Wrestling Championships
summary: In the Under-17 Asian Wrestling Championships, India’s women wrestlers delivered a strong performance, winning a total of 10 medals, including 2 gold, 3 silver, and 5 bronze in Da Nang, Vietnam. Leading the charge, Diksha in the 43kg category and Garima in the...
body: In the Under-17 Asian Wrestling Championships, India’s women wrestlers delivered a strong performance, winning a total of 10 medals, including 2 gold, 3 silver, and 5 bronze in Da Nang, Vietnam. Leading the charge, Diksha in the 43kg category and Garima in the 73kg category clinched gold medals with dominant victories in their respective finals. India added three silver medals through Nikita in the 49kg, Antra in the 65kg, and Taniya in the 69kg categories. Taniya went down in a closely contested final against Khadisha Flyuk, while Nikita and Antra also finished runners-up in their title bouts. The bronze medal winners were Palak in the 40kg, Anamika in the 46kg, Akshra in the 53kg, Sakshi in the 57kg, and Manya in the 61kg categories. Meanwhile, in the U-23 Asian Wrestling Championships, India’s men’s freestyle team was crowned overall champion, lifting the team title with a dominant performance. India topped the freestyle standings ahead of Kyrgyzstan and Kazakhstan, who finished second and third respectively.

title: Indian women beat Bangladesh 3-0 to top Group B in SAFF Women’s Championship
summary: Indian women’s football team defeated defending champions Bangladesh 3-0 in Margao, Goa, yesterday to top Group B of the SAFF Women’s Championship 2026. Pyari Xaxa, Lynda Kom, and Malavika P scored a goal each for world No. 69 India. Earlier last week, India t...
body: Indian women’s football team defeated defending champions Bangladesh 3-0 in Margao, Goa, yesterday to top Group B of the SAFF Women’s Championship 2026. Pyari Xaxa, Lynda Kom, and Malavika P scored a goal each for world No. 69 India. Earlier last week, India thrashed the Maldives 11-0 in their opening match of the tournament. Yet to concede a goal in the campaign, India, five-time SAFF Women’s Championship winners, will face Group A runners-up Bhutan in the semi-finals on Wednesday. World No. 112 Bangladesh, winners of the last two editions, will take on Group A winners Nepal in the other semi-final.

title: Mogali Venkatram wins India’s first gold at Asian Under-20 Athletics Championships
summary: In the Asian Under-20 Athletics Championships, India’s Mogali Venkatram clinched gold in the men’s 800 metres in Hong Kong, earning the country’s first gold medal in the event at this year’s competition. India also impressed in the relay events. The women’s 4×...
body: In the Asian Under-20 Athletics Championships, India’s Mogali Venkatram clinched gold in the men’s 800 metres in Hong Kong, earning the country’s first gold medal in the event at this year’s competition. India also impressed in the relay events. The women’s 4×400 metres team of Bhumika, Tahura, Sehnoor and Neeru won gold and set a new championship record with a time of 3 minutes 38.07 seconds. The women’s 4×100 metres quartet of Kajal, Bhavana, Aarti and Nipam secured silver, clocking 45.05 seconds to establish a new junior national record. The men’s 4×400 metres team of Piyush Raj, Sayed Sabeer, Ranjith Kumar and Mohammed Ashfaq claimed bronze, also setting a new junior national record with a time of 3 minutes 5.54 seconds. The championships are serving as a qualifying event for the World Under-20 Athletics Championships, scheduled to be held in Eugene, Oregon, in the United States from the 5th to the 9th of August.

title: Royal Challengers Bengaluru beat Gujarat Titans by five wickets to retain IPL title
summary: In cricket, defending champions Royal Challengers Bengaluru successfully retained the IPL title, defeating Gujarat Titans by five wickets in the final at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad last night. Put in to bat first, Gujarat Titans posted 155 for eigh...
body: In cricket, defending champions Royal Challengers Bengaluru successfully retained the IPL title, defeating Gujarat Titans by five wickets in the final at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad last night. Put in to bat first, Gujarat Titans posted 155 for eight in the stipulated 20 overs, with the help of Washington Sundar’s unbeaten 50 off 37 balls. For Bengaluru, Rasikh Salam Dar picked up three wickets, while Bhuvneshwar Kumar and Josh Hazlewood claimed two wickets each. In reply, Royal Challengers Bengaluru comfortably chased down the target, scoring 161 for five in 18 overs, powered by Virat Kohli’s unbeaten 75 off 42 balls. For Gujarat Titans, Rashid Khan scalped two wickets. Kohli has been named the player of the match. Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has been named the most valuable player of the season. He also won the Orange Cap for scoring 776 runs, becoming the youngest-ever recipient of the Orange Cap and rewriting the record books at just 15 years and 65 days of age. Kagiso Rabada claimed the Purple Cap for taking 29 wickets in the season. Manish Pandey won the Catch of the Season award for his stunning effort to dismiss Tim David with a blinder at backward point. Mohammed Siraj was awarded for bowling the most dot balls in the season, delivering 172 dot balls. Sai Sudharsan received the award for hitting the most fours in the season, with a tally of 75 boundaries. Vaibhav Sooryavanshi also bagged multiple honours, including Emerging Player of the Season, Super Striker of the Season with a strike rate of 237.3, and Super Sixes of the Season with 72 sixes.

title: PM Modi congratulates Indian contingent for 19 medals at Asian U20 Athletics Championships
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has congratulated the Indian contingent at the 22nd Asian U20 Athletics Championships for winning 19 medals, including 10 Golds. In a social media post, Mr Modi said this outstanding performance reflects the determination and excel...
body: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has congratulated the Indian contingent at the 22nd Asian U20 Athletics Championships for winning 19 medals, including 10 Golds. In a social media post, Mr Modi said this outstanding performance reflects the determination and excellence of India’s young athletes. He hoped that these achievements will inspire many more young Indians to pursue sports in the years to come.

title: India storms into SAFF Women’s Championship 2026 semi-finals
summary: In football, India has stormed into the semi-finals of the SAFF Women’s Championship 2026. The Indian team defeated defending champions Bangladesh 3-0 at Pandit Jawaharlal Nehru Stadium in Margao, Goa, yesterday to top Group B of the Championship. Pyari Xaxa, ...
body: In football, India has stormed into the semi-finals of the SAFF Women’s Championship 2026. The Indian team defeated defending champions Bangladesh 3-0 at Pandit Jawaharlal Nehru Stadium in Margao, Goa, yesterday to top Group B of the Championship. Pyari Xaxa, Lynda Kom, and Malavika P scored a goal each. India will now face Group A runners-up Bhutan in the semi-finals on Wednesday. On the other hand Bangladesh, will take on Group A winner Nepal in the other semi-final.

title: Belgium stuns Senegal 3-2 in extra-time thriller at FIFA World Cup
summary: In the FIFA World Cup, Belgium staged a stunning comeback to beat Senegal 3-2 after extra time in the Round of 32 and qualify for the Round of 16. Youri Tielemans scored a 125th-minute penalty to seal a dramatic 3-2 victory and extend Belgium’s unbeaten run...
body: In the FIFA World Cup, Belgium staged a stunning comeback to beat Senegal 3-2 after extra time in the Round of 32 and qualify for the Round of 16. Youri Tielemans scored a 125th-minute penalty to seal a dramatic 3-2 victory and extend Belgium’s unbeaten run to 17 matches. Co-hosts US registered their first win in the knockout round after 2002 this morning. They defeated Bosnia and Herzegovina 2-nil in the Round of 32 to advance into the round of 16, wherein they will take on Belgium. Last night, England defeated the Democratic Republic of the Congo 2-1 in Atlanta, with Harry Kane scoring twice in the final 15 minutes. England will face co-hosts Mexico in the Round of 16 on July 6. In another Round 32 clash Mexico beat Ecuador 2-0 in Mexico City to reach the Round of 16. France defeated Sweden 3-0 in New York, while Norway edged Ivory Coast 2-1 in Texas to book their place in the last 16.

title: Men’s Cricket: First T20 International between India and England abandoned due to rain
summary: In Men’s Cricket, the first T20 International between India and England was abandoned due to rain in Chester-le-Street today. The five-match series now stands level at 0-0. Opting to bat first, India scored 189 for 7 in stipulated 20 overs, with skipper...
body: In Men’s Cricket, the first T20 International between India and England was abandoned due to rain in Chester-le-Street today. The five-match series now stands level at 0-0. Opting to bat first, India scored 189 for 7 in stipulated 20 overs, with skipper Shreyas Iyer scoring the highest 68 runs off 47 balls. Opening batter Abhishek Sharma also scored 59 runs, while Shivam Dubey remained not out at 42 runs. For England, Saqib Mahmood bagged 3 wickets. However, hosts England didn’t get the opportunity to bat in the second innings as rain paused the match. Both teams will now face each other in second T20 in Manchester on Saturday, while the third match will be played in Nottingham on 7th of July. Bristol and Southampton will host the final two matches of the T20 series, which concludes on 11th of July. Following this, India will face England in a three-match ODI series.

title: 135th Edition of Durand Cup to kick off July 25 in Kolkata
summary: The 135th Edition of Durand Cup will kick off from July 25 in Kolkata and will be held across Ranchi, Imphal, Shillong and Guwahati till August 23. Kolkata will once again serve as the tournament’s hub, hosting the opening match, two quarter-finals, one...
body: The 135th Edition of Durand Cup will kick off from July 25 in Kolkata and will be held across Ranchi, Imphal, Shillong and Guwahati till August 23. Kolkata will once again serve as the tournament’s hub, hosting the opening match, two quarter-finals, one semi-final and the final at Vivekananda Yuba Bharati Krirangan and Kishore Bharati Krirangan. Jharkhand capital Ranchi will make its debut as a Durand Cup host, with the Birsa Munda Stadium staging league matches from July 26 to August 16 and one quarter-final. The teams include Indian Super League clubs, I-League sides, Services teams, emerging domestic outfits and overseas teams. Defenders FC of the Sri Lankan Armed Forces and Meghalaya Premier League champions Nongkseh Sports, Social and Cultural Club are among the debutants.

title: Hockey India announces Indian Junior Men’s team for Belgium tour
summary: Hockey India has announced the 22-member Indian Junior (Under-21) Men’s Hockey Team for the upcoming exposure tour of Belgium, scheduled from 5th to 18th of July. The tour will be the team’s first assignment under newly appointed coach Frederic Soyez and will...
body: Hockey India has announced the 22-member Indian Junior (Under-21) Men’s Hockey Team for the upcoming exposure tour of Belgium, scheduled from 5th to 18th of July. The tour will be the team’s first assignment under newly appointed coach Frederic Soyez and will serve as a key preparatory phase ahead of the FIH Hockey Men’s Junior Asia Cup later this year. Defender Anmol Ekka has been entrusted with the captaincy for the upcoming exposure tour. The goalkeeping unit comprises Vivek Lakra and Kunal Tewatia. The defensive line features captain Anmol Ekka, Rohit Kullu, Chirag, Ravinder, Prasant Barla, Sanjit Tirkey and V. Manimaran. India will begin their campaign against Austria on the 7th and 8th of July. The team will then take on the hosts, Belgium, on the 10th of July; Germany on the 13th of July; Belgium again on the 14th of July; and conclude the tour against the Netherlands on the 17th of July.

title: India to take on England in first T20I of five-match series today
summary: In Men’s Cricket, India will take on England in the first T20 International of the 5-match series at the Riverside Ground in Chester-le-Street today. The match is scheduled to begin at 10 PM Indian Time. Shreyas Iyer-led Indian side will look to bounce back...
body: In Men’s Cricket, India will take on England in the first T20 International of the 5-match series at the Riverside Ground in Chester-le-Street today. The match is scheduled to begin at 10 PM Indian Time. Shreyas Iyer-led Indian side will look to bounce back from their shocking 2-0 defeat to Ireland in the last series. All eyes will be on young sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, who received his maiden senior Indian Team call-up for the Ireland series but did not get a debut in Belfast. Meanwhile, the second T20I will be played at Manchester on Saturday, while the third match will be at Nottingham on the 7th of July. Bristol and Southampton will host the final two matches of the T20 series, which concludes on the 11th of July. Following this, India will also face England in a three-match ODI series.
